metadata.title: WorkoWork Growth Actions
metadata.description: Structured professional growth activities inspired by Amazon Leadership Principles

categories.title: Confidence
categories.description: Build confidence through repeated small actions

activities.title: Ask One Meaningful Question
activities.shortDescription: Speak once during a discussion
activities.objective: Reduce hesitation while speaking in professional settings
activities.motive: Growth begins when participation becomes intentional

task.whatToDo: During a meeting or conversation, ask one genuine question.
task.howToDo: Listen carefully and identify one area where you need clarification.
task.suggestedStarter: Can you explain how this connects with our previous implementation?
task.successCriteria: You asked one meaningful question out loud.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did speaking feel easier after you started?

flashcards.title: Purpose
flashcards.content: Confidence improves through small repeated actions.

flashcards.title: Action
flashcards.content: Ask one thoughtful question.

flashcards.title: Mindset
flashcards.content: You are learning, not performing.

activities.title: Introduce Yourself First
activities.shortDescription: Take initiative to introduce yourself in a new setting
activities.objective: Overcome the hesitation of initiating social contact
activities.motive: First movers set the tone and are remembered

task.whatToDo: Be the first to introduce yourself in a meeting or group setting.
task.howToDo: State your name, role, and one thing you're working on.
task.suggestedStarter: Hi, I'm [Name], I'm working on [project] — glad to be here.
task.successCriteria: You introduced yourself before anyone introduced you.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did others respond to your introduction?

flashcards.title: Insight
flashcards.content: Being first signals confidence even when you don't feel it.

flashcards.title: Action
flashcards.content: Name, role, one sentence.

activities.title: Volunteer an Answer
activities.shortDescription: Respond to an open question in a group setting
activities.objective: Practice sharing your perspective without being prompted
activities.motive: Your ideas have value even when imperfect

task.whatToDo: When a question is posed to the room, be the first to respond.
task.howToDo: Give your best answer clearly, then invite others to add on.
task.suggestedStarter: My take is — though I'd love to hear others too.
task.successCriteria: You answered a group question without being called on.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Was your answer good enough? What would you add now?

flashcards.title: Principle
flashcards.content: Bias for Action means acting before perfection arrives.

flashcards.title: Tip
flashcards.content: An imperfect answer invites collaboration.

activities.title: Disagree Politely
activities.shortDescription: Voice a different view respectfully
activities.objective: Build comfort with constructive disagreement
activities.motive: Respectful pushback earns more respect than silence

task.whatToDo: When you genuinely disagree with a point, say so with reasoning.
task.howToDo: Acknowledge the other view, then offer your perspective with data or logic.
task.suggestedStarter: That makes sense, but I see it differently because...
task.successCriteria: You stated a disagreement and gave a reason.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did the other person react? Did the conversation improve?

flashcards.title: Key Skill
flashcards.content: Disagreement is healthy when delivered with respect.

flashcards.title: Phrase
flashcards.content: 'That makes sense, and here's another angle...' works well.

activities.title: Speak at the Start of a Meeting
activities.shortDescription: Make your first contribution within the first 5 minutes
activities.objective: Establish your presence early in group settings
activities.motive: Early participation shapes how others perceive you throughout the meeting

task.whatToDo: In your next meeting, say something meaningful in the first 5 minutes.
task.howToDo: Comment on the agenda, ask a framing question, or respond to the opener.
guidelines: Prepare one sentence before the meeting starts
task.suggestedStarter: Before we dive in, I wanted to quickly flag...
task.successCriteria: You spoke within the first 5 minutes of a meeting.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did early participation change how you felt during the rest of the meeting?

flashcards.title: Tactic
flashcards.content: Prepare one sentence before any meeting you want to be active in.

flashcards.title: Why It Works
flashcards.content: Early speaking reduces the psychological barrier for subsequent contributions.

activities.title: Own a Mistake Publicly
activities.shortDescription: Acknowledge an error in a team setting without deflecting
activities.objective: Build credibility by demonstrating accountability
activities.motive: Owning mistakes builds more trust than pretending they didn't happen

task.whatToDo: Identify a recent mistake and acknowledge it to the team or manager.
task.howToDo: State what happened, why, what you learned, and what you'll do differently.
guidelines: Focus on the fix, not the fault
task.suggestedStarter: I want to flag something I handled incorrectly last week...
task.successCriteria: You acknowledged a mistake and stated a corrective action.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did transparency change the dynamic with your team?

flashcards.title: Trust Builder
flashcards.content: Accountability is more respected than deflection.

flashcards.title: Structure
flashcards.content: What happened → Why → What I'll do differently.

activities.title: Present Your Work Unprompted
activities.shortDescription: Share a project update without being asked
activities.objective: Take initiative in showcasing your contributions
activities.motive: Visibility of your work is part of professional ownership

task.whatToDo: Share a brief update on something you're working on in a team channel or standup.
task.howToDo: Summarize progress, next steps, and any blockers in 2–3 sentences.
guidelines: Mention the impact, not just the task
task.suggestedStarter: Quick update on [project] — we've completed X and are now focused on Y.
task.successCriteria: You shared a work update proactively.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did sharing unprompted feel different from reporting when asked?

flashcards.title: Principle
flashcards.content: Ownership means communicating your work, not just doing it.

activities.title: Challenge a Process
activities.shortDescription: Raise a question about how something is done
activities.objective: Practice questioning assumptions respectfully
activities.motive: Good processes survive scrutiny; bad ones need to be challenged

task.whatToDo: Identify one recurring process that seems inefficient and raise it with your team.
task.howToDo: Frame it as a question: 'Is there a reason we do X this way?' and suggest an alternative.
task.suggestedStarter: I noticed we do X this way — I was wondering if Y might be more efficient?
task.successCriteria: You raised a process question with at least one suggested improvement.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Was the challenge welcomed? What did you learn from the response?

flashcards.title: Mindset
flashcards.content: Questioning a process is different from criticizing people.

activities.title: Request Feedback Directly
activities.shortDescription: Ask a colleague or manager for honest feedback on your work
activities.objective: Develop comfort with seeking critical input
activities.motive: Asking for feedback signals maturity and accelerates growth

task.whatToDo: Ask one person for specific feedback on a recent piece of work.
task.howToDo: Be specific about what you want feedback on — not just 'how did I do?'
task.suggestedStarter: Could you give me feedback on how I handled [specific situation]?
task.successCriteria: You received and documented specific feedback from someone you respect.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What was the most useful piece of feedback you received?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Specific questions get specific feedback.

flashcards.title: Posture
flashcards.content: Listen to understand, not to respond.

activities.title: Run a Portion of a Meeting
activities.shortDescription: Facilitate part of a team meeting
activities.objective: Practice facilitation and ownership of group time
activities.motive: Leading a conversation, even briefly, builds executive presence

task.whatToDo: Volunteer to run one section or agenda item in an upcoming meeting.
task.howToDo: Prepare talking points, manage time, and invite input from others.
task.suggestedStarter: I'll take us through [section] — here's where we are...
task.successCriteria: You facilitated a section of a meeting and kept it on track.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What would you do differently next time you facilitate?

flashcards.title: Skill
flashcards.content: Facilitation is a leadership skill, not just a management one.

activities.title: Defend Your Decision
activities.shortDescription: Stand by a decision when pushed back on
activities.objective: Practice maintaining a position under pressure
activities.motive: Confidence includes knowing when to hold your ground

task.whatToDo: When someone questions a decision you made, respond with reasoning instead of immediately conceding.
task.howToDo: Explain your thought process, acknowledge the concern, and restate your position if you still believe it.
guidelines: Be open but not a pushover
task.suggestedStarter: I hear your concern — here's why I made that call...
task.successCriteria: You defended a decision with reasoning and didn't capitulate without cause.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Was your pushback valid? Did holding your ground lead to a better outcome?

flashcards.title: Distinction
flashcards.content: Changing your mind from new data is strength. Changing it from pressure is weakness.

activities.title: Share a Half-Formed Idea
activities.shortDescription: Offer an idea before it's fully developed
activities.objective: Reduce the fear of imperfect contributions
activities.motive: Early ideas shared get refined; unexpressed ideas stay useless

task.whatToDo: In a brainstorm or team chat, share a rough idea and invite input.
task.howToDo: Frame it honestly as early-stage and ask what others think could improve it.
task.suggestedStarter: This is a rough thought, but what if we tried...
task.successCriteria: You shared an unfinished idea and received engagement on it.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did the idea improve through collaboration?

flashcards.title: Reality
flashcards.content: No idea survives first contact unchanged. Share it anyway.

activities.title: Give a 3-Minute Stand-Up
activities.shortDescription: Present a verbal team update with structure
activities.objective: Practice concise verbal communication of progress
activities.motive: Being able to summarize your work clearly is a core professional skill

task.whatToDo: Give a structured verbal update in a team standup.
task.howToDo: Use the format: Yesterday / Today / Blocker.
task.suggestedStarter: Yesterday I completed X. Today I'm working on Y. I have a blocker around Z.
task.successCriteria: You delivered a clear, structured standup under 3 minutes.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did your team have a clear picture of your work after your update?

flashcards.title: Format
flashcards.content: Yesterday / Today / Blocker is the clearest standup format.

activities.title: Make a Recommendation
activities.shortDescription: Offer a specific recommendation rather than options only
activities.objective: Build confidence in taking a definitive stance
activities.motive: Listing options without a recommendation passes the work back to others

task.whatToDo: When asked for your input on a decision, give a clear recommendation with reasoning.
task.howToDo: State your recommendation first, then the rationale, then acknowledge trade-offs.
task.suggestedStarter: My recommendation is X. Here's why...
task.successCriteria: You made a recommendation with reasoning rather than just listing options.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did others respond to a clear recommendation versus being handed a menu of options?

flashcards.title: Structure
flashcards.content: Recommendation first, rationale second, trade-offs third.

activities.title: Speak to Senior Leadership
activities.shortDescription: Directly address someone more senior than your immediate team
activities.objective: Practice communicating across hierarchy without excessive deference
activities.motive: Senior people are just people — treating them as such builds mutual respect

task.whatToDo: In a meeting or shared space with senior leadership, ask a question or make a point directly.
task.howToDo: Prepare a question relevant to the meeting context and ask it clearly.
task.suggestedStarter: I'd like to understand your perspective on...
task.successCriteria: You spoke directly to someone at least one level above your manager.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did seniority affect how you communicated? Should it have?

flashcards.title: Insight
flashcards.content: Senior leaders often appreciate directness more than excessive formality.

activities.title: Write and Send a Point-of-View Document
activities.shortDescription: Document your position on a work decision in writing
activities.objective: Practice articulating and sharing a professional stance in writing
activities.motive: A written POV shows clarity of thinking and commitment to a position

task.whatToDo: Write a 1-page document stating your view on a current work issue or decision.
task.howToDo: State the issue, your position, supporting reasoning, and known counterarguments.
task.suggestedStarter: Issue: [X]. My position: [Y]. Reasoning: [Z]. Counterargument addressed: [A].
task.successCriteria: You shared a written POV document with your team or manager.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did writing your position make it clearer or messier?

flashcards.title: Format
flashcards.content: Issue → Position → Reasoning → Counterargument.

activities.title: Ask for a Stretch Opportunity
activities.shortDescription: Request a task that's beyond your current scope
activities.objective: Practice advocating for your own growth
activities.motive: Growth doesn't come to those who wait for it to be assigned

task.whatToDo: Ask your manager for a task or responsibility beyond your current role.
task.howToDo: Frame it as a learning opportunity and show you've thought about what you'd bring to it.
guidelines: Be specific about what you want
Offer to do it alongside current work
task.suggestedStarter: I'd like to take on [X]. I think I could contribute by doing [Y].
task.successCriteria: You formally requested a stretch assignment.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What was the response? What did the ask itself teach you about your goals?

flashcards.title: Truth
flashcards.content: Stretch opportunities rarely come without asking for them.

activities.title: Lead a Retrospective
activities.shortDescription: Facilitate a team retrospective after a project or sprint
activities.objective: Practice structured facilitation of honest team reflection
activities.motive: Teams that reflect systematically improve faster than those that don't

task.whatToDo: Volunteer to run a retrospective for your team.
task.howToDo: Use Start / Stop / Continue format. Capture action items at the end.
task.suggestedStarter: Let's take stock of how this went — I'll capture what we share.
task.successCriteria: You facilitated a retro with at least 3 concrete follow-up actions documented.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you learn about your team that you didn't know before?

flashcards.title: Format
flashcards.content: Start / Stop / Continue → Action Items.

activities.title: Deliver Difficult News
activities.shortDescription: Communicate a setback or bad news to a stakeholder
activities.objective: Build confidence in delivering honest, timely bad news
activities.motive: Transparency in bad times is what defines trustworthy professionals

task.whatToDo: When there's a setback, delay, or failure, communicate it proactively with context.
task.howToDo: State the situation clearly, explain the root cause, and present your recovery plan.
task.suggestedStarter: I want to flag something important — [situation]. Here's what happened and what I'm doing...
task.successCriteria: You proactively communicated a setback with a recovery plan before being asked.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did early transparency affect the stakeholder's reaction?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Bad news shared early is always better than bad news discovered late.

activities.title: Give a 5-Minute Lightning Talk
activities.shortDescription: Deliver a brief, structured presentation to your team
activities.objective: Practice concise and structured public speaking
activities.motive: Speaking well in short bursts is harder and more impactful than long presentations

task.whatToDo: Prepare and deliver a 5-minute talk on a topic you know well.
task.howToDo: Use the format: Problem → Insight → So What. Use no more than 3 slides.
task.suggestedStarter: What I want you to walk away with today is...
task.successCriteria: You delivered a 5-minute talk and stayed on time.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What would you cut or sharpen if you gave this talk again?

flashcards.title: Structure
flashcards.content: Problem → Insight → So What.

activities.title: Negotiate a Deadline
activities.shortDescription: Push back on an unrealistic timeline professionally
activities.objective: Practice professional boundary-setting around scope and time
activities.motive: Accepting unrealistic deadlines and failing is worse than negotiating upfront

task.whatToDo: When given a deadline that's unachievable, negotiate it with reasoning.
task.howToDo: Explain your current workload, estimate what's realistic, and propose an alternative.
task.suggestedStarter: Given X and Y, I can realistically deliver this by [date]. Can we align on that?
task.successCriteria: You negotiated a deadline and reached an agreed-upon timeline.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did the negotiation damage or strengthen the relationship?

flashcards.title: Principle
flashcards.content: A negotiated deadline you meet beats an accepted one you miss.

activities.title: Present to an External Stakeholder
activities.shortDescription: Lead a section of a client or partner-facing meeting
activities.objective: Practice professional presence in external-facing settings
activities.motive: External trust is built one interaction at a time

task.whatToDo: Volunteer to present a section of a meeting with an external stakeholder.
task.howToDo: Prepare thoroughly, anticipate their concerns, and communicate clearly.
task.suggestedStarter: I'll walk you through what we've built and what it means for your team...
task.successCriteria: You led a section of an external-facing meeting successfully.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What would you do differently based on their reactions?

flashcards.title: Prep Rule
flashcards.content: Anticipate your audience's top 3 questions before you start.

activities.title: Escalate a Risk Proactively
activities.shortDescription: Raise a project risk before it becomes a crisis
activities.objective: Build comfort with upward communication of risk
activities.motive: Escalating early is a sign of judgment, not weakness

task.whatToDo: Identify a risk in your current work and escalate it to the right person before it materializes.
task.howToDo: Document the risk, its likelihood, its impact, and your recommended mitigation.
task.suggestedStarter: I want to flag a potential risk: [X]. Here's what I think we should do...
task.successCriteria: You escalated a risk with a written summary and proposed mitigation.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Was the risk taken seriously? What would have happened if you hadn't flagged it?

flashcards.title: Format
flashcards.content: Risk → Likelihood → Impact → Mitigation.

activities.title: Interview a Subject-Matter Expert
activities.shortDescription: Conduct a structured conversation to extract expertise
activities.objective: Develop the skill of structured knowledge extraction
activities.motive: Knowing how to learn from experts is a multiplier for your own expertise

task.whatToDo: Schedule and conduct a 30-minute interview with an expert in a domain relevant to your work.
task.howToDo: Prepare 5–7 specific questions. Take notes. Summarize what you learned afterward.
task.suggestedStarter: Thank you for making time — I'd love to understand how you think about [X].
task.successCriteria: You conducted the interview and shared a written summary with your team.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What surprised you most? What assumptions did the conversation challenge?

flashcards.title: Best Practice
flashcards.content: Prepare questions before expert conversations. Ad-hoc wastes their time and yours.

activities.title: Lead a Cross-Team Initiative
activities.shortDescription: Drive a project that involves people outside your immediate team
activities.objective: Practice leading without direct authority
activities.motive: Cross-functional leadership is the most realistic form of leadership in modern orgs

task.whatToDo: Identify an improvement that spans two teams and volunteer to lead it.
task.howToDo: Get buy-in from both sides, establish a communication rhythm, and drive it to completion.
guidelines: Start with alignment on the problem
task.suggestedStarter: I've identified something that affects both teams — I'd like to lead fixing it.
task.successCriteria: You led a cross-team initiative to a defined milestone.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What made leading across teams harder than within your own team?

flashcards.title: Key Insight
flashcards.content: Cross-team leadership requires more communication, not less.

activities.title: Deliver a Keynote-Style Update to Leadership
activities.shortDescription: Present a quarterly or project summary to senior stakeholders
activities.objective: Practice high-stakes presentation delivery
activities.motive: Your work only matters to leadership if they understand it

task.whatToDo: Prepare and deliver a structured summary of your team's work to senior leadership.
task.howToDo: Lead with outcomes and impact, then dive into approach. End with asks.
task.suggestedStarter: Here's what we set out to do, what we achieved, and what we need next.
task.successCriteria: You presented to leadership and fielded questions confidently.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Which questions caught you off guard? How will you prepare for them next time?

flashcards.title: Structure
flashcards.content: Outcomes first. Approach second. Asks third.

activities.title: Challenge a Senior Decision
activities.shortDescription: Formally raise a concern about a decision made above you
activities.objective: Practice principled upward dissent
activities.motive: Good organizations need people who speak truth to power

task.whatToDo: When you believe a decision made above you is wrong, raise it formally with evidence.
task.howToDo: Request time with the decision-maker, present your concern with data, and commit to the outcome.
guidelines: Commit once a decision is made
task.suggestedStarter: I want to share a concern about this decision — can I walk you through my reasoning?
task.successCriteria: You raised your concern formally and documented the outcome.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Was the challenge received constructively? What would you do differently?

flashcards.title: Commit
flashcards.content: Disagree and Commit: you can push back before — but fully commit after.

activities.title: Run a High-Stakes Working Session
activities.shortDescription: Facilitate a structured session on a complex, contested topic
activities.objective: Practice holding group attention and driving to decisions under pressure
activities.motive: Complex problems require skilled facilitation to reach resolution

task.whatToDo: Design and run a working session where a key decision must be reached.
task.howToDo: Set a clear objective, time-box each section, surface disagreements, and close with a decision.
task.suggestedStarter: By the end of this session, we need to decide X. Here's how we'll get there.
task.successCriteria: The session ended with a documented decision and clear next steps.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Where did the session nearly go off track? How did you recover it?

flashcards.title: Facilitation Rule
flashcards.content: State the objective at the start and the decision at the end.

activities.title: Write and Publish a Team Lessons-Learned Document
activities.shortDescription: Synthesize a team's learnings into a shareable document
leadershipPrinciples: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
activities.objective: Capture and share institutional knowledge
activities.motive: Teams that document what they learn accelerate the next generation

task.whatToDo: After a major project, write a lessons-learned document and share it organization-wide.
task.howToDo: Cover: what worked, what didn't, root causes, and recommendations for future teams.
guidelines: Make it useful to someone who wasn't there
task.suggestedStarter: This document captures what Team X learned during [project] — the good and the difficult.
task.successCriteria: The document was published and at least one other team referenced it.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What would you have done differently if you'd read this document before starting the project?

flashcards.title: Structure
flashcards.content: What worked → What didn't → Root causes → Recommendations.

activities.title: Pitch a Strategic Initiative to Leadership
activities.shortDescription: Present a new organizational initiative for formal approval
activities.objective: Practice high-level strategic communication and persuasion
activities.motive: The ability to sell ideas upward is what separates individual contributors from leaders

task.whatToDo: Develop and pitch a strategic initiative to at least one VP or director-level leader.
task.howToDo: Write a 1-pager with: problem statement, proposed solution, expected impact, resource ask, and risks.
guidelines: Lead with customer or business impact
task.suggestedStarter: I'd like to share an initiative I believe could meaningfully improve [outcome].
task.successCriteria: You presented the pitch and received a formal response, even if it was a no.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did the reaction reveal about organizational priorities?

flashcards.title: Pitch Format
flashcards.content: Problem → Solution → Impact → Ask → Risks.

activities.title: Say Hello to Someone New
activities.shortDescription: Greet a colleague you've never spoken to
activities.objective: Break the ice with an unfamiliar colleague
activities.motive: Every professional relationship starts with a first exchange

task.whatToDo: Say hello to one person at work you've never spoken to.
task.howToDo: Greet them, ask a simple open question, and listen.
guidelines: Don't force it into a pitch
task.suggestedStarter: Hey, I don't think we've met — I'm [Name].
task.successCriteria: You had a short, genuine exchange with someone new.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you learn about them?

flashcards.title: Start
flashcards.content: Small talk builds large trust over time.

activities.title: Follow Up After a Meeting
activities.shortDescription: Send a follow-up message to someone you just met
activities.objective: Reinforce new connections through timely follow-up
activities.motive: Follow-through separates meaningful connections from forgettable encounters

task.whatToDo: Within 24 hours of meeting someone new, send a follow-up message.
task.howToDo: Reference something specific from your conversation.
task.suggestedStarter: Great to meet you today — I found your point about X really interesting.
task.successCriteria: You sent a specific follow-up within 24 hours.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did they respond? What might have made it more memorable?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Follow up within 24 hours while the conversation is still fresh.

activities.title: Ask About Someone's Career Path
activities.shortDescription: Show genuine curiosity about how a colleague got to their role
activities.objective: Build relationships through genuine interest
activities.motive: Understanding someone's journey reveals what drives them

task.whatToDo: Ask a colleague about how they ended up in their current role.
task.howToDo: Ask open questions and listen without redirecting to yourself.
task.suggestedStarter: How did you get into this kind of work?
task.successCriteria: You learned something meaningful about a colleague's background.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What surprised you about their path?

flashcards.title: Key
flashcards.content: People remember those who genuinely listened.

activities.title: Introduce Two People
activities.shortDescription: Connect two colleagues who would benefit from knowing each other
activities.objective: Build value in your network by creating connections
activities.motive: The most valuable networkers are connectors, not collectors

task.whatToDo: Identify two people who should know each other and make the introduction.
task.howToDo: Send a brief email or message explaining why each would benefit.
task.suggestedStarter: [Name A], meet [Name B] — I think you'd both benefit from connecting around [topic].
task.successCriteria: You made an introduction and the two people connected.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did the introduction lead anywhere? What made it valuable?

flashcards.title: Principle
flashcards.content: Connectors create more value in networks than collectors do.

activities.title: Attend a Professional Event
activities.shortDescription: Show up to an internal or external professional gathering
activities.objective: Expand your professional network beyond your immediate team
activities.motive: Exposure to different perspectives accelerates your thinking

task.whatToDo: Attend an internal talk, external meetup, or industry event.
task.howToDo: Introduce yourself to at least 2 new people. Take one actionable note.
guidelines: Follow up with one person afterward
task.suggestedStarter: I'm [Name] from [company/team] — what brings you to this event?
task.successCriteria: You attended, met 2 new people, and followed up with one.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What was the most useful conversation you had?

flashcards.title: Tip
flashcards.content: Set a small goal before attending any event — it makes it worth going.

activities.title: Share a Useful Resource
activities.shortDescription: Forward an article, tool, or report that helps a colleague
activities.objective: Add value to your network proactively
activities.motive: Generosity with knowledge builds long-term professional goodwill

task.whatToDo: Share one relevant article, tool, or report with a colleague, unprompted.
task.howToDo: Include one sentence on why it's relevant to them specifically.
task.suggestedStarter: Thought of you when I saw this — might be useful for what you're working on.
task.successCriteria: You shared a resource that was acknowledged as useful.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did they find it helpful? Would you share differently next time?

flashcards.title: Value
flashcards.content: Sharing useful things builds a reputation as someone worth knowing.

activities.title: Request an Informational Interview
activities.shortDescription: Ask someone in a role you're curious about for 20 minutes of their time
activities.objective: Learn about different roles through direct conversation
activities.motive: Informational interviews teach you what job descriptions never will

task.whatToDo: Reach out to someone in a role or company you're curious about and ask for a 20-minute call.
task.howToDo: Prepare 5 focused questions. Come to learn, not to impress.
task.suggestedStarter: I'm exploring [area] and your background in [X] caught my attention — would you be open to 20 minutes?
task.successCriteria: You completed an informational interview and sent a follow-up.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you learn that you couldn't have found online?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Ask specific questions. Generic questions get generic answers.

activities.title: Congratulate Someone on a Public Win
activities.shortDescription: Acknowledge a colleague's achievement publicly or directly
activities.objective: Build goodwill through recognition of others
activities.motive: Recognizing others' wins costs nothing and builds real relationships

task.whatToDo: When you see a colleague win or achieve something, acknowledge it specifically.
task.howToDo: Post a comment in a team channel or send a direct message with specific praise.
guidelines: Be specific about what they did
task.suggestedStarter: Saw what you did with [project] — really impressive how you handled [specific aspect].
task.successCriteria: You recognized someone's achievement with a specific, genuine message.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did they respond? When did someone last do this for you?

flashcards.title: Impact
flashcards.content: Specific recognition is 10x more meaningful than generic praise.

activities.title: Have Lunch with Someone Outside Your Team
activities.shortDescription: Share a meal with a colleague from a different function
activities.objective: Build cross-functional understanding through informal conversation
activities.motive: The strongest professional relationships are built informally

task.whatToDo: Invite a colleague from a different team to lunch.
task.howToDo: Let the conversation flow naturally. Learn about their work without making it an interview.
guidelines: Don't talk shop the whole time
Ask about their goals and challenges
task.suggestedStarter: I've been meaning to connect outside of meetings — want to grab lunch this week?
task.successCriteria: You had lunch with someone outside your team and learned something new about their work.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you learn about the company from their perspective?

flashcards.title: Insight
flashcards.content: Informal conversations reveal what formal meetings hide.

activities.title: Offer Help Before Being Asked
activities.shortDescription: Proactively offer support to a colleague who seems stretched
activities.objective: Build reciprocal professional relationships through proactive support
activities.motive: Professional relationships are built on demonstrated reliability, not just conversation

task.whatToDo: Identify a colleague who appears overloaded and offer specific help.
task.howToDo: Offer to take on one concrete task, not just a vague 'let me know if you need anything.'
guidelines: Don't create more work for them
task.suggestedStarter: You seem stretched with [project] — I can take [specific task] off your plate if that helps.
task.successCriteria: You offered and delivered specific help to a stretched colleague.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did they respond? What did this do for the relationship?

flashcards.title: Distinction
flashcards.content: 'Let me know if you need help' is not an offer — a specific offer is.

activities.title: Connect With Someone on LinkedIn Meaningfully
activities.shortDescription: Send a personalized connection request to a relevant professional
activities.objective: Build your external professional network intentionally
activities.motive: Your network extends beyond your current company

task.whatToDo: Send a personalized LinkedIn connection request to someone you met or admire professionally.
task.howToDo: Write a 2–3 sentence note explaining who you are and why you want to connect.
task.suggestedStarter: Hi [Name], I came across your work on [X] — I'm in a similar space and would love to connect.
task.successCriteria: You sent a personalized request and it was accepted.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did the personalization make a difference in whether they accepted?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: A message that could be sent to anyone will be ignored by everyone.

activities.title: Share Your Work in a Professional Community
activities.shortDescription: Post or share something you've built or learned in a professional forum
activities.objective: Build professional visibility beyond your organization
activities.motive: Sharing your work is how your professional reputation compounds

task.whatToDo: Write a short post, article, or comment sharing a lesson or project in a professional community.
task.howToDo: Share one insight from your recent work in plain language. Add what you'd do differently.
guidelines: Don't make it a press release
task.suggestedStarter: One thing I learned this month working on [X]...
task.successCriteria: You published content in a professional community and received at least one substantive response.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What response surprised you most?

flashcards.title: Compound
flashcards.content: Every post shared builds a professional reputation that compounds over time.

activities.title: Map Your Professional Network
activities.shortDescription: Visualize who is in your network and identify gaps
activities.objective: Understand the current state of your professional network
activities.motive: You can't strengthen a network you haven't mapped

task.whatToDo: Draw or list your professional network by category: mentors, peers, cross-functional, external.
task.howToDo: Use a simple grid or list. Note who you haven't spoken to in 3+ months.
task.suggestedStarter: Who in my network can I learn from, refer to, and be referred by?
task.successCriteria: You created a network map and identified 2–3 specific gaps to address.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Which relationships have you been neglecting?

flashcards.title: Framework
flashcards.content: Map by: Mentors / Peers / Cross-functional / External.

activities.title: Reconnect With a Dormant Contact
activities.shortDescription: Reach out to someone you haven't spoken to in 6+ months
activities.objective: Maintain long-term relationships through periodic re-engagement
activities.motive: Dormant relationships are easier to reactivate than building new ones from scratch

task.whatToDo: Message someone you haven't spoken to in 6+ months with a genuine reason to reconnect.
task.howToDo: Reference something relevant — their recent work, a shared memory, or a timely topic.
task.suggestedStarter: I saw your post on [X] and it reminded me we hadn't caught up in a while — how are things?
task.successCriteria: You reactivated a dormant relationship and had a real exchange.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How easy was it to pick up where you left off?

flashcards.title: Truth
flashcards.content: Maintaining a relationship is far easier than rebuilding one.

activities.title: Ask for a Professional Referral
activities.shortDescription: Request a referral or recommendation from someone in your network
activities.objective: Practice leveraging your network for professional opportunities
activities.motive: Most opportunities come through relationships, not job boards

task.whatToDo: Ask someone you've worked with to refer or recommend you for an opportunity, role, or project.
task.howToDo: Make the ask specific — tell them exactly what you're looking for and why you're reaching out to them.
guidelines: Be specific about what you want
Make it easy to say yes
task.suggestedStarter: Given your knowledge of my work on [X], I'd love your recommendation for [opportunity].
task.successCriteria: You made a specific ask and received a response (even if they couldn't help).

activities.reflectionPrompt: What made the ask easy or hard for them?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Vague asks get vague responses. Be specific.

activities.title: Host a Knowledge Exchange
activities.shortDescription: Organize a peer session where people share what they know
activities.objective: Create value in your network by facilitating shared learning
activities.motive: The best networkers create gatherings, not just attend them

task.whatToDo: Organize a small knowledge-sharing session with 3–5 colleagues on a topic each of them knows well.
task.howToDo: Give everyone 5–10 minutes to share. Capture key takeaways.
task.suggestedStarter: I want to run a quick 'teach each other' session — who's in?
task.successCriteria: You organized and ran the session with at least 3 participants.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you learn that surprised you most?

flashcards.title: Insight
flashcards.content: Organizing a learning session gives you access to everyone's knowledge at once.

activities.title: Build a Relationship with Your Skip-Level Manager
activities.shortDescription: Initiate a meaningful conversation with your manager's manager
activities.objective: Develop visibility and perspective from senior layers of the organization
activities.motive: Knowing how the organization thinks at higher levels improves your work at every level

task.whatToDo: Request a 20-minute conversation with your skip-level manager — not about a specific problem, just to connect.
task.howToDo: Prepare 2–3 thoughtful questions about their priorities and how your work fits into the bigger picture.
guidelines: Don't position it as a complaint session
Ask about their goals, not just yours
Follow up with a summary of what you took away
task.suggestedStarter: I'd love 20 minutes to better understand how our team's work fits into your broader priorities.
task.successCriteria: You had the conversation and learned something you didn't know before.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did their perspective differ from your direct manager's?

flashcards.title: Purpose
flashcards.content: Skip-level conversations reveal organizational context you can't get at eye level.

activities.title: Give a Substantive Reference
activities.shortDescription: Provide a detailed professional reference for a colleague
activities.objective: Practice articulating someone else's professional strengths accurately
activities.motive: Giving great references is both generous and a signal of your own judgment

task.whatToDo: Agree to provide a professional reference and write a detailed, specific endorsement.
task.howToDo: Include specific examples, situations, and outcomes. Avoid generic praise.
guidelines: Be honest about growth areas too if asked
task.suggestedStarter: One specific example of [Name]'s impact was when they...
task.successCriteria: You delivered a reference that included at least two specific examples.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did articulating their strengths teach you about what to look for in others?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Specific examples are what make references credible.

activities.title: Mentor Someone Junior for a Month
activities.shortDescription: Commit to supporting a junior colleague for 4 weeks
leadershipPrinciples: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
activities.objective: Build a sustained mentoring relationship
activities.motive: The most powerful network investments are long-term

task.whatToDo: Identify a junior colleague and offer to meet weekly for a month to support their growth.
task.howToDo: Each session: listen to their challenges, share relevant experience, and hold them accountable to one goal.
guidelines: End each session with an action item for them
task.suggestedStarter: I'd like to support your development over the next month — want to try a few sessions?
task.successCriteria: You completed 4 sessions and the mentee made one observable progress.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did mentoring teach you about your own professional assumptions?

flashcards.title: Insight
flashcards.content: Mentoring others teaches you what you didn't know you knew.

activities.title: Speak at an Internal Event
activities.shortDescription: Share your expertise in a company-wide or multi-team forum
activities.objective: Build professional visibility through public knowledge sharing
activities.motive: Speaking at internal events signals confidence and builds cross-company reputation

task.whatToDo: Submit a proposal or volunteer to speak at an internal lunch-and-learn, town hall, or team event.
task.howToDo: Prepare a 15–20 minute talk with a clear takeaway. Practice at least twice.
guidelines: Open with a question or story
task.suggestedStarter: What I want everyone to walk away understanding is...
task.successCriteria: You delivered the talk and received questions or feedback from the audience.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What question surprised you most?

flashcards.title: Prep
flashcards.content: One clear takeaway is better than five mediocre points.

activities.title: Build a Relationship with a Vendor or Partner
activities.shortDescription: Develop a genuine professional relationship with an external partner
activities.objective: Build relationships beyond your organization's walls
activities.motive: Vendor and partner relationships done well create long-term organizational value

task.whatToDo: Invest in a genuine relationship with a key vendor or partner contact — not just transactional exchanges.
task.howToDo: Schedule a check-in, ask about their challenges, share your team's goals.
guidelines: Don't only call when you need something
task.suggestedStarter: I wanted to check in beyond our usual project conversations — how are things on your end?
task.successCriteria: You had a non-transactional conversation with an external partner.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did shifting the dynamic from transactional to relational change the conversation?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: People don't work hard for vendors. They work hard for people they respect.

activities.title: Create a Professional Newsletter
activities.shortDescription: Publish a curated newsletter for your professional network
activities.objective: Build professional visibility through consistent value delivery
activities.motive: A newsletter is a long-term investment in your professional reputation

task.whatToDo: Create and send the first edition of a professional newsletter to your network.
task.howToDo: Curate 3–5 useful resources with one sentence of your own commentary on each.
guidelines: Add your own lens to each item
Commit to a frequency you can maintain
task.suggestedStarter: Here's what I've been reading and thinking about this month...
task.successCriteria: You published and sent the first edition to at least 10 people.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What kind of content got the most responses?

flashcards.title: Value
flashcards.content: Commentary is what makes a curated newsletter worth reading.

activities.title: Participate in a Professional Panel
activities.shortDescription: Join a panel discussion at a professional event or webinar
activities.objective: Practice articulating professional perspectives in a public forum
activities.motive: Panel discussions build external credibility and expand your professional reach

task.whatToDo: Accept an invitation to join or proactively apply to participate in a professional panel.
task.howToDo: Prepare your top 3 points on the topic. Practice articulating them under 2 minutes each.
task.suggestedStarter: From my experience working on [X], the thing that's often missed is...
task.successCriteria: You participated in the panel and made at least 2 substantive contributions.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did other panelists say that changed your thinking?

flashcards.title: Prep
flashcards.content: Know your 3 unique points before any panel. Don't just respond — contribute.

activities.title: Build a Cross-Company Peer Group
activities.shortDescription: Form a regular peer learning group with professionals from different organizations
activities.objective: Create a lasting professional community beyond your company
activities.motive: Peer groups accelerate development in ways internal communities can't

task.whatToDo: Recruit 4–6 peers from different companies and establish a regular monthly call.
task.howToDo: Set a rotating agenda: each person brings one challenge or topic. Rotate facilitation.
guidelines: Keep the group small and curated
task.suggestedStarter: I'm starting a small peer group for [role/level] — monthly, informal, real conversations. Want in?
task.successCriteria: You held the first call with 4+ members and scheduled the second.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you learn from someone in a completely different company context?

flashcards.title: Format
flashcards.content: Small + curated + rotating agenda = a peer group worth keeping.

activities.title: Sponsor Someone's Opportunity
activities.shortDescription: Use your network to open a door for a junior colleague
leadershipPrinciples: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
activities.objective: Move from mentoring to sponsorship — creating opportunities for others
activities.motive: Sponsorship is more powerful than mentorship: you risk your reputation to open doors

task.whatToDo: Nominate, refer, or advocate for a junior colleague for a visible opportunity you have access to.
task.howToDo: Use your relationship capital to make an explicit recommendation on their behalf.
guidelines: Only sponsor those you genuinely believe in
Follow up to close the loop
task.suggestedStarter: I'd like to put forward [Name] for this — here's why they're ready.
task.successCriteria: You made a formal recommendation for a junior colleague for an opportunity.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What does it feel like to spend relationship capital on someone else's behalf?

flashcards.title: Distinction
flashcards.content: Mentors talk to you. Sponsors talk about you.

activities.title: Negotiate a Partnership
activities.shortDescription: Facilitate a formal partnership or collaboration between your team and another
activities.objective: Build partnerships that create organizational value
activities.motive: Strategic partnerships compound value in ways individual efforts cannot

task.whatToDo: Identify an opportunity for a formal collaboration with another team or organization and drive it to agreement.
task.howToDo: Define the mutual value proposition, establish roles, and document the agreement.
task.suggestedStarter: I think there's a real opportunity here for both teams — let me walk you through what I'm seeing.
task.successCriteria: You reached a documented agreement for a formal collaboration.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What made the other party say yes?

flashcards.title: Foundation
flashcards.content: Partnerships only last when both sides see the value.

activities.title: Keynote or Speak at an External Conference
activities.shortDescription: Apply to and speak at an external professional conference
activities.objective: Build an external professional brand through public speaking
activities.motive: Conference speaking multiplies your professional reach beyond your organization

task.whatToDo: Submit a proposal to speak at an external conference or professional event.
task.howToDo: Develop a clear talk with one strong thesis. Practice extensively.
guidelines: Have a clear point of view
task.suggestedStarter: I want to talk about [thesis] because most people in this field believe [opposite].
task.successCriteria: You submitted a proposal and (if accepted) delivered the talk.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What aspect of your proposal made it stand out — or not?

flashcards.title: Pitch Rule
flashcards.content: A talk proposal with a clear point of view gets accepted. Generic ones don't.

activities.title: Build a Formal Advisory Relationship
activities.shortDescription: Establish yourself as an advisor to someone outside your organization
activities.objective: Contribute expertise to organizations beyond your own
activities.motive: Advisory relationships build external credibility and refine your own thinking

task.whatToDo: Formalize an advisory relationship with a startup, nonprofit, or peer organization in your area of expertise.
task.howToDo: Define the scope, frequency, and mutual expectations of the relationship.
guidelines: Add specific value — not just general advice
task.suggestedStarter: I'd like to offer structured support on [specific area] — here's what I can commit to.
task.successCriteria: You formalized an advisory relationship with documented expectations.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What does advising from outside teach you about your own organization?

flashcards.title: Value
flashcards.content: Advising others forces you to articulate expertise you've internalized.

activities.title: Build a Community of Practice
activities.shortDescription: Create and sustain a professional learning community in your domain
leadershipPrinciples: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
activities.objective: Create lasting value for a professional community
activities.motive: Communities of practice outlast any individual contribution

task.whatToDo: Launch a structured, recurring community around a professional topic: a guild, SIG, or CoP.
task.howToDo: Define the mission, recruit founding members, and run the first 3 sessions.
guidelines: Define what 'good' looks like for the community
Recruit members who will contribute, not just consume
task.suggestedStarter: I'm starting a community for [domain professionals] — our first session is [date].
task.successCriteria: You ran 3 sessions with at least 6 consistent participants.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What made members want to come back?

flashcards.title: Sustainability
flashcards.content: A community survives when members own it, not just the founder.

activities.title: Publish Thought Leadership
activities.shortDescription: Write and publish an original piece of professional thought leadership
activities.objective: Establish an authoritative professional perspective in your domain
activities.motive: Published thinking is a permanent, compounding professional asset

task.whatToDo: Write and publish a substantive article, essay, or report on a topic where you have original insight.
task.howToDo: Take a clear, arguable position. Support it with evidence or experience. Publish it publicly.
guidelines: Take a position most wouldn't publicly take
task.suggestedStarter: Most people in [field] believe X. Here's why I think the opposite is true — and what we should do instead.
task.successCriteria: You published a piece that sparked substantive professional discussion.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What responses challenged your thinking most?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Thought leadership requires a point of view, not just information.

activities.title: Write a Clear Email
activities.shortDescription: Send an email with one subject, one ask, and no fluff
activities.objective: Practice crisp, purposeful written communication
activities.motive: Unclear emails waste time — yours and everyone else's

task.whatToDo: Write and send an email that has one subject, one clear ask, and is under 5 sentences.
task.howToDo: State context in one sentence, the ask in one sentence, the deadline in one sentence.
guidelines: Put the ask in the first or last line
Cut anything that doesn't add value
task.suggestedStarter: Context: [X]. I need [Y] by [Z].
task.successCriteria: Your email was under 5 sentences and had a single clear ask.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did you receive a clear response? What helped?

flashcards.title: Formula
flashcards.content: Context + Ask + Deadline = a clear email.

activities.title: Listen Without Interrupting
activities.shortDescription: Let someone finish their entire thought before responding
activities.objective: Practice active, patient listening
activities.motive: Most people are thinking about their response while others are still talking

task.whatToDo: In your next conversation, consciously let the other person finish completely before you speak.
task.howToDo: Wait 2 seconds after they stop before responding. Notice what you would have interrupted.
task.suggestedStarter: N/A — this is about what you don't say.
task.successCriteria: You let someone complete their thought in full at least 3 times in one conversation.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you notice that you would have missed if you'd interrupted?

flashcards.title: Insight
flashcards.content: The 2-second pause after someone stops speaking is where real listening happens.

activities.title: Summarize a Meeting in Writing
activities.shortDescription: Send a written summary of decisions and action items after a meeting
activities.objective: Build the habit of capturing and sharing meeting outcomes
activities.motive: Meetings without summaries are conversations — not decisions

task.whatToDo: Within 30 minutes of a meeting, send a written summary to all attendees.
task.howToDo: Cover: decisions made, action items (owner + deadline), and any open questions.
guidelines: Keep it to a half page
task.suggestedStarter: Summary from [meeting]: Decisions: [X]. Actions: [Y] (Owner, Deadline). Open: [Z].
task.successCriteria: You sent a summary within 30 minutes with decisions and owners.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did the summary surface anything that wasn't clear during the meeting?

flashcards.title: Format
flashcards.content: Decisions / Actions (Owner + Deadline) / Open Questions.

activities.title: Give a Specific Compliment
activities.shortDescription: Recognize someone's work with a specific, genuine comment
activities.objective: Practice delivering meaningful recognition
activities.motive: Vague praise is forgotten. Specific praise is remembered and repeated.

task.whatToDo: Give one person a specific compliment about something they did well.
task.howToDo: Name the behavior, why it mattered, and the impact it had.
task.suggestedStarter: The way you handled [situation] — specifically [behavior] — made a real difference because [impact].
task.successCriteria: You gave one specific, situation-based compliment.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did they respond differently to specific vs general praise?

flashcards.title: Formula
flashcards.content: Behavior + Why It Mattered + Impact = meaningful recognition.

activities.title: Simplify a Complex Explanation
activities.shortDescription: Explain a technical or complex concept to a non-expert
activities.objective: Practice translating complexity into clarity
activities.motive: If you can't explain it simply, you don't fully understand it

task.whatToDo: Take a complex concept from your work and explain it to someone unfamiliar with the domain.
task.howToDo: Use an analogy. Test by asking if they could explain it back.
task.suggestedStarter: Think of it like...
task.successCriteria: The listener was able to explain the concept back to you in their own words.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Where did you lose them? What made it click?

flashcards.title: Test
flashcards.content: If they can explain it back, you explained it well.

activities.title: Ask a Clarifying Question Before Assuming
activities.shortDescription: Pause to clarify intent before reacting to ambiguous communication
activities.objective: Reduce miscommunication through deliberate clarification
activities.motive: Most workplace conflict starts with assumed intent

task.whatToDo: When you receive an ambiguous message, ask for clarification before responding based on your assumption.
task.howToDo: Ask one specific question to confirm your understanding of their intent.
task.suggestedStarter: Before I respond — just want to make sure I understand what you're asking. Are you saying [X]?
task.successCriteria: You clarified intent before responding in at least 2 exchanges.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Was your original assumption correct?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Clarify once. Don't assume and apologize later.

activities.title: Deliver Feedback Using a Structure
activities.shortDescription: Give feedback using a clear, repeatable framework
activities.objective: Practice structured, actionable feedback delivery
activities.motive: Unstructured feedback is hard to act on and often feels personal

task.whatToDo: Give feedback to a colleague using the SBI format: Situation, Behavior, Impact.
task.howToDo: Describe a specific situation, name the behavior, explain the impact on you or the team.
guidelines: End with what you'd prefer to see
task.suggestedStarter: In [situation], when you did [behavior], it resulted in [impact].
task.successCriteria: You delivered structured feedback that the recipient could act on.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did they receive it compared to past unstructured feedback?

flashcards.title: Framework
flashcards.content: Situation + Behavior + Impact = actionable feedback.

activities.title: Write a One-Pager
activities.shortDescription: Communicate a proposal or update in exactly one page
activities.objective: Practice disciplined, structured written communication
activities.motive: The constraint of one page forces clarity

task.whatToDo: Write a one-pager for a proposal, update, or decision that needs stakeholder input.
task.howToDo: Sections: Context, Problem, Proposed Solution, Expected Outcome, Ask.
guidelines: Make the ask explicit on the last line
task.suggestedStarter: Context: [X]. Problem: [Y]. Solution: [Z]. Expected Outcome: [A]. Ask: [B].
task.successCriteria: Your one-pager was exactly one page and received a substantive response.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you cut to make it fit? Did cutting improve it?

flashcards.title: Structure
flashcards.content: Context / Problem / Solution / Outcome / Ask.

activities.title: Facilitate a Decision in a Meeting
activities.shortDescription: Guide a group to reach a clear decision in a meeting
activities.objective: Practice steering conversations toward outcomes
activities.motive: Meetings that don't end with decisions waste everyone's time

task.whatToDo: In your next meeting where a decision is needed, actively drive toward a conclusion.
task.howToDo: Name the decision to be made, surface the key options, and push for a resolution.
task.suggestedStarter: I want to make sure we leave with a decision today — the question is [X]. Let's try to resolve it now.
task.successCriteria: The meeting ended with a documented decision.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What kept the group from deciding? How did you help unblock it?

flashcards.title: Facilitator Role
flashcards.content: Name the decision. Surface options. Force resolution.

activities.title: Translate Strategy into Team Action
activities.shortDescription: Take a high-level strategic goal and break it into team tasks
activities.objective: Practice bridging strategic communication and execution
activities.motive: Strategy only matters when people know what to do on Monday

task.whatToDo: Take one strategic objective from leadership and translate it into specific, assignable tasks for your team.
task.howToDo: Write a translation: '[Objective] means we specifically need to do [Task A], [Task B], [Task C].'
guidelines: Connect each task to the goal
task.suggestedStarter: The leadership priority of [X] means for our team specifically: ...
task.successCriteria: You translated one strategic objective into 3+ specific team tasks with owners.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did your translation resonate with the team? What was unclear?

flashcards.title: Bridge
flashcards.content: Strategy → Team-level tasks → Individual owners.

activities.title: Write a Status Report
activities.shortDescription: Produce a structured weekly status report for stakeholders
activities.objective: Build the habit of structured stakeholder communication
activities.motive: Stakeholders who feel informed are easier to work with

task.whatToDo: Write a weekly status report using a consistent format.
task.howToDo: Use: Accomplished / In Progress / Next Week / Risks / Asks.
task.suggestedStarter: Week of [Date]: Accomplished: / In Progress: / Next: / Risks: / Asks:
task.successCriteria: You sent a consistent status report for 3 consecutive weeks.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did consistent reporting change how stakeholders engaged with your work?

flashcards.title: Format
flashcards.content: Accomplished / In Progress / Next / Risks / Asks.

activities.title: Respond to Criticism Constructively
activities.shortDescription: Receive critical feedback without becoming defensive
activities.objective: Build emotional resilience in feedback conversations
activities.motive: How you receive feedback matters as much as how you give it

task.whatToDo: When you receive critical feedback, pause, thank them, ask a clarifying question, and reflect before responding.
task.howToDo: Practice the sequence: Pause → Acknowledge → Clarify → Reflect.
task.suggestedStarter: Thank you for raising this — can you help me understand specifically what you'd like to see differently?
task.successCriteria: You received feedback, asked a clarifying question, and didn't get defensive.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you learn by not immediately defending yourself?

flashcards.title: Sequence
flashcards.content: Pause → Acknowledge → Clarify → Reflect.

activities.title: Write a BLUF Communication
activities.shortDescription: Lead all your communications with the key point first
activities.objective: Practice Bottom Line Up Front (BLUF) communication style
activities.motive: Busy people read the first two lines. The rest is for the curious.

task.whatToDo: Write an email, Slack message, or doc where the single most important point is in the first sentence.
task.howToDo: Start with: 'The key point is [X].' Then provide context below.
task.suggestedStarter: Short version: [key point]. Here's the context: ...
task.successCriteria: You sent a BLUF communication where the most important information was in the opening line.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did BLUF structure change how quickly people responded?

flashcards.title: BLUF
flashcards.content: Bottom Line Up Front: the conclusion always comes first.

activities.title: Give Real-Time Feedback
activities.shortDescription: Provide feedback immediately after an observable behavior
activities.objective: Practice timely, specific feedback in the moment
activities.motive: Delayed feedback is disconnected feedback

task.whatToDo: Within 5 minutes of observing a behavior worth commenting on, give feedback directly.
task.howToDo: Acknowledge what you saw, state the impact, and (if constructive) suggest an alternative.
guidelines: Don't wait for a review cycle
task.suggestedStarter: I just wanted to say — what you did in [situation] was really effective because...
task.successCriteria: You gave feedback within 5 minutes of the observable behavior.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did immediacy make the feedback land differently?

flashcards.title: Timing
flashcards.content: Feedback is most useful when connected to the moment it refers to.

activities.title: Communicate Across Cultures
activities.shortDescription: Adjust your communication style for a colleague from a different cultural context
activities.objective: Develop cultural communication awareness
activities.motive: What is direct in one culture is rude in another

task.whatToDo: Identify one communication habit you have that may land differently with a cross-cultural colleague and adjust it.
task.howToDo: Research their communication culture or ask directly. Then adapt one interaction.
task.suggestedStarter: I want to make sure I'm communicating in a way that works for you — is there anything I should know?
task.successCriteria: You made a deliberate adjustment in one cross-cultural interaction and reflected on the outcome.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did adjusting your style cost you? What did it gain?

flashcards.title: Principle
flashcards.content: Communication is only complete when the receiver understands it as you intended.

activities.title: Conduct a Communication Audit
activities.shortDescription: Review your last 10 messages and assess their clarity
activities.objective: Build self-awareness about your communication patterns
activities.motive: You can't improve what you haven't examined

task.whatToDo: Review your last 10 emails or messages and assess each for clarity, tone, and outcome.
task.howToDo: For each, ask: Was the ask clear? Was the tone appropriate? Did I get the response I needed?
task.suggestedStarter: What would a stranger think my intent was based on this message alone?
task.successCriteria: You identified one specific communication pattern to improve and started doing it differently.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What surprised you most about your own communication style?

flashcards.title: Self-Audit
flashcards.content: Review messages as a stranger would read them — is the ask clear?

activities.title: Present Data With a Narrative
activities.shortDescription: Turn a data set into a story that drives action
activities.objective: Practice transforming data into insights and decisions
activities.motive: Data without narrative is noise. Data with narrative is a decision.

task.whatToDo: Take a data set or report and build a short narrative that explains what it means and what to do.
task.howToDo: Use: What the data shows → Why it matters → What we should do.
guidelines: Lead with the insight, not the data
Pick one clear story to tell
task.suggestedStarter: The data shows [X]. This matters because [Y]. Here's what I recommend we do: [Z].
task.successCriteria: You presented data with a clear narrative that resulted in a decision or action.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Would the team have reached the same conclusion without the narrative?

flashcards.title: Structure
flashcards.content: Data shows → Why it matters → What to do.

activities.title: Write a Press Release for Your Project
activities.shortDescription: Draft a mock press release announcing your project's outcome
activities.objective: Practice communicating impact in external-facing language
activities.motive: Writing a press release forces you to focus on outcome, not process

task.whatToDo: Write a mock press release announcing your project as if it were ready to go public.
task.howToDo: Cover: headline, problem solved, solution, user benefit, quote from 'stakeholder', and call to action.
guidelines: Write for someone who knows nothing about your project
Focus on the benefit, not the features
task.suggestedStarter: [Company] Launches [Product] to Help [User] Achieve [Outcome].
task.successCriteria: You wrote a press release that passed review from one non-expert colleague.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did writing the press release reveal about what your project is actually for?

flashcards.title: Amazon Trick
flashcards.content: Write the press release before building the product. It clarifies the goal.

activities.title: Run an Async Stand-Up
activities.shortDescription: Replace a recurring sync meeting with an async communication format
activities.objective: Practice designing efficient asynchronous communication
activities.motive: Async communication respects time and scales across time zones

task.whatToDo: Design and run an async stand-up for your team using a written format.
task.howToDo: Create a shared template: Yesterday / Today / Blockers. Ask team to fill it before a set time.
guidelines: Resolve blockers in threads, not full meetings
task.suggestedStarter: Let's try an async standup this week — please fill in [template] by 9am each day.
task.successCriteria: You ran an async standup for at least 5 consecutive days.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did async communication surface anything that sync meetings were hiding?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Async is not lazy — it is disciplined communication.

activities.title: Write a Difficult Message
activities.shortDescription: Draft a message that addresses tension, disappointment, or a sensitive topic
activities.objective: Build skill in navigating difficult written communication
activities.motive: Avoiding difficult conversations damages relationships. Addressing them builds trust.

task.whatToDo: Identify a message you've been avoiding and write a draft today.
task.howToDo: Start with intent: 'I'm writing this because I care about [X].' Then state the issue. End with what you need.
task.suggestedStarter: I want to address something that's been on my mind because it matters to our work together...
task.successCriteria: You drafted and sent the difficult message.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What happened? What were you avoiding by not sending it sooner?

flashcards.title: Start
flashcards.content: State your intent first. It changes how everything after it is received.

activities.title: Facilitate an Active Listening Exercise
activities.shortDescription: Run a structured listening exercise with your team
activities.objective: Improve team-level listening skills through structured practice
activities.motive: Listening is a team sport — improving it together compounds the benefit

task.whatToDo: Run a listening exercise with your team where speakers are reflected back before others respond.
task.howToDo: Each speaker shares. The next person must paraphrase before adding their own view.
task.suggestedStarter: We're going to try something: before responding, paraphrase what you just heard.
task.successCriteria: You ran the exercise and the team debriefed what changed.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did team members learn about how well they actually listen?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Paraphrase before responding — it forces real listening.

activities.title: Craft a Persuasive Memo
activities.shortDescription: Write a memo that convinces stakeholders to change a decision
activities.objective: Practice high-stakes persuasive writing
activities.motive: Persuasion through well-reasoned writing is a senior leadership skill

task.whatToDo: Write a memo to convince decision-makers to change or reconsider a decision.
task.howToDo: Lead with the problem, present your evidence, address objections, and make a clear ask.
guidelines: Acknowledge the current decision's merits first
task.suggestedStarter: This memo recommends reconsidering [X] for the following reasons...
task.successCriteria: You sent the memo and received a substantive response.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What argument was hardest to make? What was most persuasive?

flashcards.title: Structure
flashcards.content: Problem → Evidence → Objections addressed → Ask.

activities.title: Create a Communication Plan for a Project
activities.shortDescription: Design a structured communication strategy for a significant initiative
activities.objective: Practice systematic stakeholder communication management
activities.motive: Projects fail on communication as often as they fail on execution

task.whatToDo: Design a communication plan for a current or upcoming project.
task.howToDo: Map: stakeholders, what they need to know, when, and in what format.
task.suggestedStarter: Stakeholder: / Needs to know: / Frequency: / Format:
task.successCriteria: You created a communication plan and executed it for at least 2 weeks.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What stakeholder was most surprised by the proactive communication?

flashcards.title: Map
flashcards.content: Who / What they need / When / How — map all four.

activities.title: Write an Executive Summary
activities.shortDescription: Condense a complex project or analysis into a 2-paragraph summary for senior leadership
activities.objective: Practice extreme brevity with maximum clarity
activities.motive: Leaders make decisions from summaries, not reports

task.whatToDo: Write a 2-paragraph executive summary of a current project or analysis.
task.howToDo: Para 1: What we did and found. Para 2: What it means and what we recommend.
task.suggestedStarter: Para 1: We investigated [X] and found [Y]. Para 2: This means [Z]. We recommend [action].
task.successCriteria: The executive summary was read and acted upon by at least one senior stakeholder.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you cut that turned out to not matter?

flashcards.title: Format
flashcards.content: Para 1: What we did and found. Para 2: What it means and what to do.

activities.title: Write a Newsletter to Your Team
activities.shortDescription: Create a recurring internal update that keeps your team informed and engaged
activities.objective: Practice consistent, engaging team communication
activities.motive: Teams that feel informed perform better and trust more

task.whatToDo: Design and send the first edition of a team newsletter.
task.howToDo: Cover: wins this week, upcoming priorities, shoutouts, and one interesting link or reading.
guidelines: Include at least one personal touch
task.suggestedStarter: Team Update — Week of [Date]: Wins: / Coming Up: / Shoutout to: / Worth Reading:
task.successCriteria: You published 3 consecutive newsletters and the team referenced them.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What section got the most engagement?

flashcards.title: Sections
flashcards.content: Wins / Upcoming / Shoutouts / Reading.

activities.title: Communicate Vision to Your Team
activities.shortDescription: Articulate a compelling vision for what your team is working toward
activities.objective: Practice inspiring communication that connects work to purpose
activities.motive: Teams with a clear vision outperform those just executing tasks

task.whatToDo: Write and share a 1-paragraph vision statement for what your team is building and why it matters.
task.howToDo: Answer: Who does this serve? What problem does it solve? What does success look like in 2 years?
guidelines: Speak to purpose, not just process
Invite team input to refine it
task.suggestedStarter: In two years, our team will have [achieved X] for [audience] so that [impact].
task.successCriteria: You shared a vision statement and the team could repeat it back.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What changed in team energy or direction after sharing the vision?

flashcards.title: Vision Test
flashcards.content: Can your team recite it from memory? If not, simplify it.

activities.title: Conduct a Communication Debrief After a Crisis
activities.shortDescription: Lead a structured review of how communication was handled during a difficult situation
activities.objective: Build organizational communication resilience through structured reflection
activities.motive: Crisis communication failures are predictable and preventable

task.whatToDo: After a project challenge, outage, or difficult situation, run a formal communication debrief.
task.howToDo: Review: what was communicated, to whom, when, and what was missing. Document improvements.
guidelines: Define what good would have looked like
task.suggestedStarter: Let's review how we communicated during [event] — not to blame, but to improve.
task.successCriteria: You ran the debrief and documented 3 specific communication improvements.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What would have happened if communications had failed completely?

flashcards.title: Debrief Frame
flashcards.content: What was communicated / What was missing / What we'd do differently.

activities.title: Mediate a Communication Breakdown
activities.shortDescription: Help two colleagues resolve a miscommunication directly
activities.objective: Practice neutral facilitation in conflict resolution
activities.motive: Unresolved communication breakdowns compound into organizational dysfunction

task.whatToDo: When two colleagues are at odds due to miscommunication, facilitate a structured resolution conversation.
task.howToDo: Get both parties to state their understanding. Find the gap. Help them agree on the path forward.
