guidelines: Focus on moving forward, not assigning blame
task.suggestedStarter: I want to help both of you get to a clear understanding — let's each share what we heard.
task.successCriteria: You facilitated a resolution conversation that ended with documented agreement.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What was the root of the breakdown? Was it content or communication style?

flashcards.title: Mediation Rule
flashcards.content: State the gap clearly. Then focus only on closing it.

activities.title: Design a Team Communication Charter
activities.shortDescription: Co-create a written agreement on how your team communicates
activities.objective: Establish shared communication norms for sustained team performance
activities.motive: Implicit communication norms cause conflict. Explicit ones prevent it.

task.whatToDo: Facilitate a team session to create a communication charter: norms for meetings, messages, and decisions.
task.howToDo: Cover: expected response times, meeting norms, async vs sync decisions, and escalation paths.
guidelines: Get team buy-in, not just manager sign-off
task.suggestedStarter: Let's agree on how we communicate — not just what we communicate.
task.successCriteria: You produced a communication charter signed off by the whole team.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Which norm was hardest to agree on? Why?

flashcards.title: Sections
flashcards.content: Response times / Meeting norms / Async vs sync / Escalation.

activities.title: Give a TED-Style Talk on a Professional Topic
activities.shortDescription: Deliver a polished, idea-driven talk for a professional audience
activities.objective: Master high-impact public speaking on an original professional insight
activities.motive: The ability to communicate ideas that change how others think is a rare and powerful skill

task.whatToDo: Prepare and deliver a polished talk with one clear, original idea and a compelling narrative arc.
task.howToDo: Open with a story or question. Develop the idea. Close with the implication for the audience.
task.suggestedStarter: The thing I want you to think differently about after today is...
task.successCriteria: You delivered the talk and at least one audience member described a shift in their thinking.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What made the audience lean in?

flashcards.title: Talk Structure
flashcards.content: Open with story → Develop the idea → Close with implication.

activities.title: Learn Your Teammates' Names
activities.shortDescription: Know every team member's name and one fact about them
activities.objective: Build the foundation of team awareness
activities.motive: You can't build a team with people you don't know

task.whatToDo: Learn the name and one personal or professional fact about every person on your immediate team.
task.howToDo: If you can't name them all, review your team directory or Slack and take notes.
guidelines: Learn one real fact — not just job title
task.suggestedStarter: Hi [Name], I realized I don't know much about what you work on — what's your focus right now?
task.successCriteria: You can name every teammate and one fact about each.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Was there anyone you'd been ignoring without realizing it?

flashcards.title: Foundation
flashcards.content: Team trust starts with knowing who is on the team.

activities.title: Celebrate a Team Win
activities.shortDescription: Acknowledge and mark a team achievement together
activities.objective: Build team morale through shared recognition
activities.motive: Teams that pause to celebrate retain energy for what comes next

task.whatToDo: Recognize a team win — big or small — in a group setting.
task.howToDo: Call out what was achieved, who contributed, and why it matters.
task.suggestedStarter: Before we move on — I want to take a moment to recognize what we just did...
task.successCriteria: You led a team recognition moment and at least 3 people were named.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did the team respond? Did recognition energize or feel awkward?

flashcards.title: Why
flashcards.content: Teams that celebrate wins sustain energy through challenges.

activities.title: Share a Non-Work Fact About Yourself
activities.shortDescription: Humanize yourself to your team with a personal disclosure
activities.objective: Lower team social barriers through appropriate self-disclosure
activities.motive: Teams perform better when members see each other as people, not just roles

task.whatToDo: Share one non-work fact about yourself during a team meeting or chat.
task.howToDo: Pick something genuine, low-stakes, and relatable.
task.suggestedStarter: Random fact about me you probably don't know — I...
task.successCriteria: You shared a personal fact and others reciprocated.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did sharing change how you felt in the team environment?

flashcards.title: Science
flashcards.content: Appropriate self-disclosure builds trust and team cohesion.

activities.title: Check In Before Diving In
activities.shortDescription: Start meetings with a brief personal check-in round
activities.objective: Build team connection through brief human moments in work meetings
activities.motive: A 2-minute check-in dramatically improves meeting engagement and honesty

task.whatToDo: Start your next team meeting with a one-word or one-sentence check-in from each person.
task.howToDo: Ask everyone: 'In one word, how are you showing up today?' Go around the room.
guidelines: Don't comment on each — just collect and acknowledge
task.suggestedStarter: Before we start — quick round: one word for how you're feeling today. I'll go first: [word].
task.successCriteria: You ran a check-in and everyone participated.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did knowing how people were feeling change how the meeting went?

flashcards.title: Effect
flashcards.content: A check-in makes the invisible (how people feel) visible — and that changes everything.

activities.title: Create a Team Working Agreement
activities.shortDescription: Facilitate a session to agree on how your team works together
activities.objective: Establish explicit team norms for collaboration
activities.motive: Implicit norms create conflict. Explicit agreements prevent it.

task.whatToDo: Facilitate a team session to agree on your shared working norms.
task.howToDo: Cover: meeting norms, communication preferences, feedback style, and how decisions get made.
task.suggestedStarter: Let's spend an hour agreeing on how we want to work together — not just what we're working on.
task.successCriteria: You produced a documented working agreement that the whole team signed off on.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Which norm was hardest to agree on?

flashcards.title: Principle
flashcards.content: Explicit agreements prevent conflict before it starts.

activities.title: Help Onboard a New Team Member
activities.shortDescription: Take personal ownership of welcoming and orienting a new colleague
activities.objective: Build team belonging through active onboarding
activities.motive: The first week shapes how someone feels about the team for months

task.whatToDo: When a new team member joins, take personal responsibility for their first week experience.
task.howToDo: Reach out, schedule a 1:1, introduce them to key people, and answer their questions proactively.
guidelines: Don't assume someone else will do it
Check in daily the first week
Introduce them with context, not just name
task.suggestedStarter: Welcome! I want to make sure your first week is as smooth as possible — let me walk you through how we work.
task.successCriteria: The new team member told you the first week felt welcoming and clear.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What would have made your own onboarding better?

flashcards.title: Impact
flashcards.content: How someone is onboarded determines how quickly they contribute.

activities.title: Run a Team Skills Inventory
activities.shortDescription: Map the skills and strengths across your team
activities.objective: Build awareness of team capability distribution
activities.motive: You can't use your team's strengths if you don't know what they are

task.whatToDo: Create a simple inventory of skills across your team — who's strong at what.
task.howToDo: Ask each team member to list their top 3 skills and one skill they're developing. Compile and share.
guidelines: Use it to assign work more intentionally
task.suggestedStarter: I want us to know each other's strengths better — can you each share 3 you're confident in?
task.successCriteria: You created and shared a team skills map.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What skill gap surprised you most?

flashcards.title: Use
flashcards.content: Skills maps improve task assignment and team self-awareness.

activities.title: Organize a Team Retrospective
activities.shortDescription: Facilitate a team retrospective at the end of a sprint or project
activities.objective: Build team improvement habits through structured reflection
activities.motive: Teams that review their work improve faster than those that only do the work

task.whatToDo: Run a team retrospective using the Start / Stop / Continue format.
task.howToDo: Give everyone time to write individually first. Then discuss and vote on top items. End with 3 actions.
guidelines: Allow anonymous input if trust is low
task.suggestedStarter: Let's look at what's working and what isn't — so we can get better intentionally.
task.successCriteria: You ran a retro and produced 3 documented actions with owners.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did the team respond to structured reflection vs. informal venting?

flashcards.title: Format
flashcards.content: Start / Stop / Continue → Vote → 3 Actions with owners.

activities.title: Resolve a Team Tension Directly
activities.shortDescription: Address an interpersonal tension within your team before it becomes a conflict
activities.objective: Build skill in handling interpersonal friction constructively
activities.motive: Unaddressed tension metastasizes. Addressed tension often becomes trust.

task.whatToDo: Identify a tension between team members and address it directly — don't wait for it to explode.
task.howToDo: Speak privately with those involved. Name the pattern you're seeing. Facilitate a resolution.
task.suggestedStarter: I've noticed some friction between us around [topic] — I'd like to work through it so we're aligned.
task.successCriteria: You addressed the tension directly and reached a documented resolution.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What would have happened if you'd waited another week?

flashcards.title: Truth
flashcards.content: Tensions don't resolve themselves. They either get addressed or they compound.

activities.title: Pair on a Task With a Colleague
activities.shortDescription: Work side-by-side with a colleague on a shared task
activities.objective: Build collaboration skills through intentional joint work
activities.motive: Collaboration produces better outcomes and stronger relationships than working in silos

task.whatToDo: Invite a colleague to work on a task together — in the same room or on a shared screen.
task.howToDo: One person leads, the other observes and contributes. Switch halfway through.
guidelines: Actually collaborate — don't just report to each other
task.suggestedStarter: Want to work on [task] together this afternoon? I think two heads would help here.
task.successCriteria: You completed a task together and both contributed meaningfully.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you do better together than either would have done alone?

flashcards.title: Why
flashcards.content: Pair work builds both the product and the relationship at the same time.

activities.title: Create a Team Wiki or Knowledge Base Entry
activities.shortDescription: Document team knowledge so others can benefit
activities.objective: Build team knowledge infrastructure
activities.motive: Knowledge that lives only in one person's head is a fragile asset

task.whatToDo: Write one knowledge base article on something only you (or a few people) know.
task.howToDo: Cover: what it is, how it works, and common edge cases. Ask a teammate to review.
guidelines: Write for someone who doesn't know
task.suggestedStarter: Here's how [process/tool/decision] works — and why it was set up this way.
task.successCriteria: You published a knowledge article that a teammate referenced within 2 weeks.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What knowledge in your team is still not documented?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: If only one person knows it, it's a risk — not a feature.

activities.title: Give Unsolicited Positive Feedback to a Peer
activities.shortDescription: Proactively recognize a peer's contribution in a meaningful way
activities.objective: Build a culture of peer recognition
activities.motive: Recognition from peers is often more meaningful than recognition from managers

task.whatToDo: Send or deliver specific positive feedback to a peer, unsolicited and unprompted.
task.howToDo: Name the behavior, the situation, and the impact it had on you or the team.
guidelines: Don't wait for a review cycle
task.suggestedStarter: I wanted to say — what you did with [X] made a real difference. Specifically, [Y].
task.successCriteria: You gave specific, unsolicited positive feedback to a peer.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did they respond? When did someone last do this for you?

flashcards.title: Impact
flashcards.content: Peer recognition in the moment is more powerful than end-of-year reviews.

activities.title: Facilitate a Team Problem-Solving Session
activities.shortDescription: Run a structured session to solve a shared team challenge
activities.objective: Build facilitation skills in problem-solving contexts
activities.motive: Structured problem-solving produces better outcomes than freeform discussion

task.whatToDo: Facilitate a team session to solve one specific challenge using a structured format.
task.howToDo: Use: Problem definition → Root cause → Options → Decision → Owner.
guidelines: Define the problem first — don't jump to solutions
End with one clear decision and owner
task.suggestedStarter: Before we brainstorm solutions, let's make sure we agree on the actual problem.
task.successCriteria: You facilitated the session and left with a documented decision and owner.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How different was the outcome versus an unstructured discussion?

flashcards.title: Process
flashcards.content: Problem → Root cause → Options → Decision → Owner.

activities.title: Run a Blameless Post-Mortem
activities.shortDescription: Lead a structured review of what went wrong without assigning blame
activities.objective: Build a culture of psychological safety and continuous improvement
activities.motive: Blame-focused post-mortems produce defensiveness. Blameless ones produce learning.

task.whatToDo: Facilitate a post-mortem after a project failure or incident.
task.howToDo: Cover: timeline, what went wrong, contributing factors, and systemic improvements. Zero blame on individuals.
guidelines: Make clear upfront: no blame, only learning
End with action items that prevent recurrence
task.suggestedStarter: We're here to understand what happened so we can improve — not to assign fault.
task.successCriteria: You ran a blameless post-mortem with 3+ systemic improvements documented.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What systemic issue did you uncover that individual blame would have hidden?

flashcards.title: Principle
flashcards.content: Blame stops learning. Blameless reviews create it.

activities.title: Build a Team Dashboard
activities.shortDescription: Create a shared visibility tool for team progress and blockers
activities.objective: Increase team transparency and alignment through shared visibility
activities.motive: Shared visibility reduces status meetings and surfaces blockers faster

task.whatToDo: Build a simple team dashboard that shows current priorities, progress, and blockers.
task.howToDo: Use a spreadsheet, project management tool, or simple doc. Update it together daily or weekly.
task.suggestedStarter: I want to create a single place where we can all see where things stand.
task.successCriteria: Your team used the dashboard for 3+ consecutive weeks.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did the dashboard surface that wasn't visible before?

flashcards.title: Value
flashcards.content: Shared visibility reduces status meetings and surprises.

activities.title: Run a Team Health Check
activities.shortDescription: Survey your team on collaboration, clarity, and morale
activities.objective: Build data-driven understanding of team health
activities.motive: You can't improve team health without measuring it

task.whatToDo: Create and run a short team health survey. Share the results honestly.
task.howToDo: Ask: How clear are our goals? How is collaboration? What's getting in the way? Then discuss.
guidelines: Keep it anonymous if trust is low
Share all results — not just good ones
Act on at least one thing
task.suggestedStarter: I'd like to run a quick team health check — honest answers help us improve.
task.successCriteria: You ran the survey, shared results, and acted on one finding.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What result surprised you most?

flashcards.title: Principle
flashcards.content: Team health is measurable. Measure it.

activities.title: Pair Junior and Senior Team Members on a Project
activities.shortDescription: Deliberately create mentored collaboration opportunities
leadershipPrinciples: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
activities.objective: Build deliberate mentoring into team workflow
activities.motive: Pairing is the fastest way to transfer tacit knowledge

task.whatToDo: Assign a junior and senior team member to work jointly on a real project with a defined mentoring element.
task.howToDo: Define what the senior is coaching. Give the junior increasing ownership over time.
task.suggestedStarter: I'm pairing you two on [project] — [Senior], I'd like you to coach [Junior] on [skill].
task.successCriteria: The pair completed a project milestone and the junior could independently repeat the skill.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did the senior learn from the junior?

flashcards.title: Truth
flashcards.content: Mentoring is often as educational for the mentor as the mentee.

activities.title: Design a Team Ritual
activities.shortDescription: Create a recurring team practice that builds connection or celebrates progress
activities.objective: Build team culture through intentional recurring practices
activities.motive: Team rituals create identity and belonging that sustains through difficult periods

task.whatToDo: Propose and implement one recurring team ritual — a practice that happens regularly and strengthens the team.
task.howToDo: Examples: weekly wins thread, Friday kudos, end-of-sprint demo, learning lunch. Commit for 4 weeks.
guidelines: Get team input on the format
task.suggestedStarter: I want to try something to help us connect around [goal] — here's what I'm thinking.
task.successCriteria: You ran the ritual for 4+ consecutive weeks and the team actively participated.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What changed in the team's energy or cohesion?

flashcards.title: Design Rule
flashcards.content: A good ritual is easy to participate in and hard to stop.

activities.title: Navigate a Team Conflict to Resolution
activities.shortDescription: Mediate a substantive disagreement within your team to a productive outcome
activities.objective: Build skill in facilitating productive conflict resolution
activities.motive: Conflict avoided becomes dysfunction. Conflict navigated becomes trust.

task.whatToDo: When a genuine disagreement exists within your team, facilitate a structured resolution process.
task.howToDo: Get both positions stated clearly. Find common ground. Drive to a decision all commit to.
guidelines: Let both sides speak fully first
Find the root disagreement — don't fix symptoms
task.suggestedStarter: We have a real disagreement here — let's work through it so we can move forward together.
task.successCriteria: The conflict reached a documented resolution that all parties committed to.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did the conflict reveal about your team's underlying assumptions?

flashcards.title: Process
flashcards.content: State positions → Find common ground → Drive to commitment.

activities.title: Advocate for a Team Member in a Review
activities.shortDescription: Formally speak up for a team member's contributions in a performance context
activities.objective: Build advocacy skills within performance systems
activities.motive: Contributions that aren't advocated for often aren't rewarded

task.whatToDo: In a performance review, calibration, or project debrief, explicitly advocate for a team member's specific contributions.
task.howToDo: Name specific work, outcomes, and impact. Be detailed and quantify where possible.
task.suggestedStarter: I want to specifically highlight [Name]'s contribution to [X] — here's the impact: [Y].
task.successCriteria: You formally advocated for a team member with specific, documented contributions.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did your advocacy change the outcome for them?

flashcards.title: Advocacy Rule
flashcards.content: Vague advocacy is ineffective. Specific, quantified advocacy changes decisions.

activities.title: Run a Cross-Team Collaboration Sprint
activities.shortDescription: Organize a short joint project between two teams
activities.objective: Build relationships and capabilities across team boundaries
activities.motive: Cross-team collaboration builds organizational resilience

task.whatToDo: Identify a shared problem between two teams and run a 1–2 week joint sprint to solve it.
task.howToDo: Define scope, assign joint roles, hold daily check-ins, and demo the outcome.
guidelines: Assign a DRI from each team
task.suggestedStarter: I think both teams can benefit from working on [problem] together — want to run a short sprint?
task.successCriteria: The joint sprint produced a working outcome and both teams used it.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did the two teams learn from each other's working style?

flashcards.title: Setup
flashcards.content: Shared goal + joint DRIs + daily rhythm + joint demo = successful cross-team sprint.

activities.title: Build Psychological Safety in Your Team
activities.shortDescription: Implement practices that make it safe to speak up, fail, and dissent
activities.objective: Create the conditions for high team performance
activities.motive: The number one predictor of team effectiveness is psychological safety

task.whatToDo: Implement 3 specific practices over 4 weeks to build psychological safety in your team.
task.howToDo: Examples: model vulnerability, publicly reward speaking up, create a safe space for dissent.
guidelines: Reward the behavior you want to see
React well to the first risk-taker
task.suggestedStarter: I want us to be a team where people feel safe to say hard things — here's how I want to start.
task.successCriteria: You can point to 3 specific changes in team behavior over 4 weeks.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Who spoke up that hadn't before? What made it possible?

flashcards.title: Research
flashcards.content: Google's Project Aristotle found psychological safety is the #1 predictor of team success.

activities.title: Redesign a Team Meeting That Isn't Working
activities.shortDescription: Audit and restructure a recurring team meeting for better outcomes
activities.objective: Practice process redesign applied to team collaboration
activities.motive: Bad meetings are expensive and demoralizing — improving them is high-leverage

task.whatToDo: Identify one recurring meeting that isn't effective. Redesign it and run the new version.
task.howToDo: Audit: why does it exist? What outcomes should it produce? Redesign for those outcomes.
guidelines: Start with the outcome, not the format
Challenge whether the meeting needs to exist
Get team input on the redesign
task.suggestedStarter: I want to make our [meeting] actually useful — let's redesign it together.
task.successCriteria: You ran the redesigned meeting format for 3 sessions and the team rated it better.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What meeting should you cut entirely?

flashcards.title: First Question
flashcards.content: Before redesigning a meeting, ask if it needs to exist at all.

activities.title: Conduct Team Individual Development Conversations
activities.shortDescription: Have dedicated career development conversations with each team member
leadershipPrinciples: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
activities.objective: Invest systematically in team member development
activities.motive: Teams that feel invested in stay longer and contribute more

task.whatToDo: Have a dedicated 30-minute career development conversation with each team member.
task.howToDo: Ask: Where do you want to be in 2 years? What do you need to get there? How can I help?
guidelines: This is not a performance review
Follow up with a specific action
task.suggestedStarter: I want to talk about your growth — not your current projects, but where you want to go.
task.successCriteria: You completed a development conversation with each team member and followed up on one action each.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you learn about your team's ambitions that surprised you?

flashcards.title: Three Questions
flashcards.content: Where do you want to go? What's in the way? How can I help?

activities.title: Co-Create a Team Vision
activities.shortDescription: Facilitate a session where the team builds its own shared vision
activities.objective: Build team ownership of collective direction
activities.motive: A vision the team builds is a vision the team believes

task.whatToDo: Facilitate a structured session where the team creates its own shared vision for the next year.
task.howToDo: Use: Where are we now? Where do we want to be? What will we need to do? Synthesize into a vision statement.
guidelines: Make it short enough to remember
task.suggestedStarter: Today we're going to build our vision together — not receive one from above.
task.successCriteria: The team produced a shared vision statement that all members can repeat from memory.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What themes emerged that leadership hadn't articulated?

flashcards.title: Insight
flashcards.content: Participation in vision creation drives ownership of vision execution.

activities.title: Implement a Peer Feedback Process
activities.shortDescription: Design and run a structured peer feedback cycle for your team
activities.objective: Build a team culture of continuous peer development
activities.motive: Peer feedback, done well, accelerates individual growth faster than manager feedback alone

task.whatToDo: Design and run a peer feedback cycle for your team — structured, safe, and actionable.
task.howToDo: Create a simple template. Give everyone time to write. Share safely. Debrief as a team.
guidelines: Follow up — don't let it sit
task.suggestedStarter: I want us to give each other structured feedback — here's the format we'll use.
task.successCriteria: All team members completed and received peer feedback and named one action to take.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What feedback pattern emerged across multiple people?

flashcards.title: Key
flashcards.content: Structure makes peer feedback safe. Structure makes it actionable.

activities.title: Build a Team Talent Pipeline
activities.shortDescription: Identify and develop the next generation of talent within your team
leadershipPrinciples: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
activities.objective: Invest in systematic team talent development
activities.motive: Teams that build talent pipelines are resilient. Those that don't are fragile.

task.whatToDo: Identify 2–3 high-potential team members and design a 90-day growth plan for each.
task.howToDo: Define: stretch goals, skills to develop, and support you'll provide. Review monthly.
guidelines: Make the plan visible to them
task.suggestedStarter: I've identified you as someone I want to invest in over the next 90 days — here's my thinking.
task.successCriteria: Each high-potential team member completed a 90-day growth plan milestone.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What blocked their growth? What unlocked it?

flashcards.title: Principle
flashcards.content: Talent pipelines don't build themselves — they require deliberate investment.

activities.title: Facilitate a Difficult Organizational Decision
activities.shortDescription: Help your team navigate a complex, high-stakes organizational choice
activities.objective: Build the skill of navigating complex decisions with teams
activities.motive: How a team makes hard decisions reveals and shapes its culture

task.whatToDo: When a genuinely difficult decision arises — with real trade-offs — facilitate the team through it to a clear choice.
task.howToDo: Name the decision, list options, identify trade-offs, get input, drive to a decision, commit.
task.suggestedStarter: This is a hard decision with real trade-offs — let's work through it together.
task.successCriteria: The team reached a documented decision with full commitment, even among those who disagreed.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What made the decision hard? What made it possible?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Once a decision is made — disagree and commit. Partial commitment is no commitment.

activities.title: Create a Succession Plan for Your Role
activities.shortDescription: Identify and prepare a successor to your current responsibilities
leadershipPrinciples: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
activities.objective: Build organizational resilience through proactive succession planning
activities.motive: The best leaders make themselves replaceable by developing those around them

task.whatToDo: Identify one person who could succeed you in your current role and create a 90-day development plan to prepare them.
task.howToDo: List your key responsibilities. For each, identify how you'd transfer knowledge. Involve them actively.
task.suggestedStarter: I want to develop you to be able to take over key parts of my role — here's where I'd like to start.
task.successCriteria: The successor could independently handle at least 50% of your key responsibilities after 90 days.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did preparing a successor reveal about the hidden work in your role?

flashcards.title: Leadership Test
flashcards.content: A great leader's team runs well whether the leader is there or not.

activities.title: Build a High-Performance Team Charter
activities.shortDescription: Create a formal, comprehensive charter for your team's culture and performance standards
activities.objective: Institutionalize team culture and standards for sustained high performance
activities.motive: High-performance teams are designed, not lucky

task.whatToDo: Co-create a comprehensive team charter covering vision, values, operating norms, and performance standards.
task.howToDo: Include: purpose, values, operating agreements, decision rights, and success metrics.
task.suggestedStarter: I want us to formally define who we are as a team and how we operate at our best.
task.successCriteria: The charter was completed with full team buy-in and referenced at least once in team decisions.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What value or norm was hardest to agree on? Why?

flashcards.title: Sections
flashcards.content: Purpose / Values / Operating agreements / Decision rights / Success metrics.

activities.title: Fix One Small Thing Uninstructed
activities.shortDescription: Spot and resolve a small issue without being asked
activities.objective: Build the habit of noticing and acting on small problems
activities.motive: Initiative starts with fixing what you see, not waiting to be told

task.whatToDo: Find one small thing that's broken, unclear, or inefficient and fix it without being asked.
task.howToDo: Could be a broken link, a missing doc, a confusing process step, or a formatting issue.
task.suggestedStarter: I noticed [issue] and went ahead and fixed it.
task.successCriteria: You identified and resolved one small issue without being asked.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What else did you notice that's broken but not your job?

flashcards.title: Mindset
flashcards.content: If you see it and you can fix it, fix it.

activities.title: Document an Undocumented Process
activities.shortDescription: Write down how something works before someone has to ask
activities.objective: Reduce institutional knowledge gaps through proactive documentation
activities.motive: Undocumented processes create bottlenecks and dependency

task.whatToDo: Choose one process that only exists in someone's head and document it.
task.howToDo: Write step-by-step instructions and share with the team.
guidelines: Get it reviewed by one person who does the process
task.suggestedStarter: I documented how we do [process] — it was only in my head until now.
task.successCriteria: You published a process doc and a teammate used it within 2 weeks.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What other knowledge lives only in people's heads?

flashcards.title: Risk
flashcards.content: Undocumented processes are single points of failure.

activities.title: Identify a Recurring Problem
activities.shortDescription: Name a pattern that keeps coming back and propose tracking it
activities.objective: Build pattern recognition and proactive problem framing
activities.motive: Recurring problems don't solve themselves — someone has to name them first

task.whatToDo: Identify one problem that keeps recurring in your work and write up a clear problem statement.
task.howToDo: Describe: what keeps happening, when, the impact, and a hypothesis for the root cause.
task.suggestedStarter: We keep running into [X] — here's my hypothesis for why and what I think we should look at.
task.successCriteria: You wrote and shared a problem statement that prompted investigation.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did naming the pattern change how others thought about the problem?

flashcards.title: Structure
flashcards.content: What keeps happening + When + Impact + Hypothesis = a good problem statement.

activities.title: Improve a Tool or Template Your Team Uses
activities.shortDescription: Make something your team uses regularly better
activities.objective: Build the habit of improving shared team resources
activities.motive: Small improvements to shared tools multiply across everyone who uses them

task.whatToDo: Identify one template, spreadsheet, or tool your team uses regularly and make it better.
task.howToDo: Add a missing column, fix a confusing section, or simplify a workflow step. Share the update.
guidelines: Share what you changed and why
task.suggestedStarter: I updated [tool] — specifically [what changed] because [why]. Let me know if it helps.
task.successCriteria: You improved a shared tool and the team adopted the change.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What improvement would have the most impact on your team's daily work?

flashcards.title: Multiplier
flashcards.content: Improving a shared tool benefits everyone who uses it — every time.

activities.title: Ask What's Coming Next
activities.shortDescription: Get ahead of upcoming work before it becomes urgent
activities.objective: Build proactive work anticipation habits
activities.motive: Reactive professionals wait for work. Proactive ones anticipate it.

task.whatToDo: Ask your manager or team what major work is coming in the next 4–6 weeks and start preparing.
task.howToDo: Ask, then create a simple prep checklist for the top item.
task.suggestedStarter: What's the biggest thing coming our way in the next 6 weeks that I should start preparing for?
task.successCriteria: You identified and took at least one preparatory action for upcoming work.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did preparing early reduce stress when the work arrived?

flashcards.title: Mindset
flashcards.content: Ask what's coming. Then prepare for it before it arrives.

activities.title: Volunteer for a Difficult Task
activities.shortDescription: Raise your hand for work others are avoiding
activities.objective: Build a reputation for taking on hard work
activities.motive: The hardest tasks are where the most growth and visibility occur

task.whatToDo: When a difficult, ambiguous, or uncomfortable task arises, volunteer for it.
task.howToDo: Raise your hand before you're asked. Clarify scope if needed, then execute.
task.suggestedStarter: I'll take that on — can we quickly align on what done looks like?
task.successCriteria: You volunteered for and completed a task others were avoiding.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did the hard task teach you that an easy task wouldn't have?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Hard tasks are where growth happens. Volunteer for them.

activities.title: Propose a Solution With Your Problem
activities.shortDescription: Never bring a problem to your manager without a proposed solution
activities.objective: Build solution-oriented problem presentation habits
activities.motive: Problem-spotters are common. Problem-solvers are rare and valued.

task.whatToDo: Before bringing a problem to a manager or team, write out at least one proposed solution.
task.howToDo: State the problem, your proposed fix, the tradeoffs, and what you need from them.
guidelines: Have at least one option ready
Ask for input, not just approval
task.suggestedStarter: I've hit [problem] — here's what I'd propose we do, and here's where I need your input.
task.successCriteria: You presented a problem with a proposed solution rather than just the problem.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did presenting a solution change the conversation?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Never arrive at a problem without a proposed path forward.

activities.title: Create a Project Timeline Unprompted
activities.shortDescription: Map out milestones for your work before being asked to
activities.objective: Build proactive project planning habits
activities.motive: Waiting to be asked for a timeline puts you behind before you start

task.whatToDo: Create a project timeline for your current work without being asked.
task.howToDo: List milestones, owners, and target dates. Share with your manager and team.
task.suggestedStarter: I've mapped out how I see [project] progressing — here's my timeline.
task.successCriteria: You created and shared a project timeline before anyone asked for one.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did creating the timeline reveal any risks you hadn't seen before?

flashcards.title: Benefit
flashcards.content: Creating a timeline before being asked reveals risks and earns trust.

activities.title: Run a Competitive Analysis
activities.shortDescription: Research and present how competitors or peers handle a problem you face
activities.objective: Build the habit of external benchmarking
activities.motive: Your toughest problems are often solved elsewhere — find the solution and adapt it

task.whatToDo: Pick one challenge your team faces and research how 2–3 competitors or comparable organizations handle it.
task.howToDo: Research, synthesize, and present your findings with recommendations.
task.suggestedStarter: I looked at how [Org A] and [Org B] handle [challenge] — here's what we can steal.
task.successCriteria: You presented a competitive analysis that led to one adopted change.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What were you doing that no one else does? Is that a strength or a gap?

flashcards.title: Mindset
flashcards.content: Your problem has probably been solved somewhere else. Find it.

activities.title: Start a Process Improvement Project
activities.shortDescription: Identify an inefficient process and lead its improvement
activities.objective: Apply initiative to systemic workflow improvement
activities.motive: Improving a process once improves every outcome that runs through it

task.whatToDo: Select one inefficient process and lead a structured effort to improve it.
task.howToDo: Document the current state, identify waste, design the new state, implement and measure.
guidelines: Involve the people who use the process
task.suggestedStarter: I've identified a process that's costing us [X] — I'd like to lead improving it.
task.successCriteria: You completed a process improvement that showed measurable improvement.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did improving the process reveal about how your team really works?

flashcards.title: Principle
flashcards.content: Process improvements are perpetual — the effort is one-time, the benefit is ongoing.

activities.title: Anticipate a Stakeholder's Question
activities.shortDescription: Prepare answers to likely questions before a stakeholder meeting
activities.objective: Build the habit of anticipating stakeholder needs
activities.motive: Professionals who anticipate questions are perceived as more credible and prepared

task.whatToDo: Before a key stakeholder meeting, list the top 5 questions they're likely to ask — and answer them.
task.howToDo: Think from their perspective: what do they care about most? What makes them nervous?
task.suggestedStarter: Before this meeting, let me think about what [stakeholder] is going to want to know.
task.successCriteria: At least 3 of your anticipated questions were actually asked.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Which anticipated question caught you off guard anyway?

flashcards.title: Exercise
flashcards.content: Before any meeting: write 5 questions the other person is likely to ask. Then answer them.

activities.title: Propose a Team Experiment
activities.shortDescription: Design and pitch a small experiment to test a hypothesis about your work
activities.objective: Build experimentation thinking into daily work
activities.motive: Testing small hypotheses is faster and cheaper than building full solutions

task.whatToDo: Identify something your team does based on assumption rather than evidence. Design a small test.
task.howToDo: State hypothesis, design the experiment, define success metric, run for 2 weeks, report results.
task.suggestedStarter: I have a hypothesis: if we try [X], we'll see [Y]. Here's how I'd test it.
task.successCriteria: You ran an experiment and reported results — regardless of outcome.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you learn that you couldn't have learned any other way?

flashcards.title: Structure
flashcards.content: Hypothesis → Experiment → Success Metric → Report.

activities.title: Create an Onboarding Guide for Your Role
activities.shortDescription: Document what someone new to your role needs to know
leadershipPrinciples: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
activities.objective: Reduce institutional knowledge dependency through proactive documentation
activities.motive: A role that can't be explained can't be handed off, scaled, or improved

task.whatToDo: Write a comprehensive onboarding guide for your specific role.
task.howToDo: Cover: key responsibilities, key relationships, key tools, common pitfalls, and first-week priorities.
guidelines: Write for someone who knows nothing about your role
task.suggestedStarter: If I needed to hand this role to someone tomorrow, here's what they'd need to know...
task.successCriteria: You produced an onboarding guide that a new team member could use immediately.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What tacit knowledge did you discover you had while writing it?

flashcards.title: Test
flashcards.content: Give it to a new team member. If they have 10+ questions, it needs more work.

activities.title: Identify and Mitigate a Project Risk Early
activities.shortDescription: Flag and act on a risk before it materializes
activities.objective: Build proactive risk identification and mitigation habits
activities.motive: Early risk response is cheaper than crisis response

task.whatToDo: Conduct a risk scan on your current project and take action on the top risk.
task.howToDo: List all risks. Rate by likelihood × impact. Develop mitigation for the top one.
task.suggestedStarter: I've done a quick risk scan on [project] and want to flag [X] before it becomes a problem.
task.successCriteria: You identified a risk, developed a mitigation, and took at least one preventive action.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did the risk materialize? What happened because of your early action?

flashcards.title: Formula
flashcards.content: Likelihood × Impact = Priority. Fix the top item first.

activities.title: Take the Lead on a Presentation
activities.shortDescription: Volunteer to own the next team presentation end-to-end
activities.objective: Build end-to-end ownership of deliverables
activities.motive: Owning a presentation develops communication, organization, and leadership simultaneously

task.whatToDo: Volunteer to own the next team presentation — structure, content, and delivery.
task.howToDo: Coordinate inputs from others, build the narrative, rehearse, and present.
task.suggestedStarter: I'd like to take this one — I'll coordinate the content and present it.
task.successCriteria: You built and delivered the presentation with positive stakeholder feedback.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What was the hardest part of owning it end-to-end?

flashcards.title: Ownership
flashcards.content: Owning a presentation means owning the outcome — not just the slides.

activities.title: Build a Business Case for a Tool or Process
activities.shortDescription: Justify an investment in something your team needs
activities.objective: Practice quantified justification of resource needs
activities.motive: Asking for resources without a business case rarely works

task.whatToDo: Build a business case for a tool, hire, or process improvement your team needs.
task.howToDo: Cover: problem, proposed solution, cost, expected benefit, and recommendation.
task.suggestedStarter: We need [X]. Here's why it's worth the investment...
task.successCriteria: You presented a business case that led to a decision — even if it was a no.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What data was most persuasive? What was missing?

flashcards.title: Format
flashcards.content: Problem / Solution / Cost / Benefit / Recommendation.

activities.title: Create a 30-60-90 Day Plan
activities.shortDescription: Map your own growth and contributions for the next three months
activities.objective: Build structured self-direction over the medium term
activities.motive: Professionals who plan their impact are more effective than those who react to it

task.whatToDo: Create a personal 30-60-90 day plan for your current role.
task.howToDo: 30 days: Learn. 60 days: Contribute. 90 days: Lead. Define specific goals for each phase.
task.suggestedStarter: Here's how I'm planning my next 90 days to maximize my contribution.
task.successCriteria: You created the plan and reviewed progress at 30, 60, and 90 days.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Where did the plan hold? Where did reality require adjustment?

flashcards.title: Framework
flashcards.content: 30: Learn. 60: Contribute. 90: Lead.

activities.title: Design a Metrics Dashboard for Your Work
activities.shortDescription: Define and track the metrics that matter most for your area
activities.objective: Build the habit of outcome-based work measurement
activities.motive: What gets measured gets managed — and what doesn't gets ignored

task.whatToDo: Identify the 3–5 most important metrics for your work and build a simple tracking dashboard.
task.howToDo: Define each metric, its source, update frequency, and what good looks like.
task.suggestedStarter: The 5 metrics that best capture whether we're winning in our work are...
task.successCriteria: You built a dashboard that you and your team used for 4+ consecutive weeks.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did having a dashboard change how you prioritized your work?

flashcards.title: Principle
flashcards.content: Pick metrics that drive behavior, not just ones that look good.

activities.title: Identify a Strategic Gap and Propose Filling It
activities.shortDescription: Spot something important that your team isn't doing and propose owning it
activities.objective: Practice strategic initiative — seeing and filling organizational gaps
activities.motive: Every organization has gaps. The people who fill them earn trust and advance.

task.whatToDo: Identify a gap in your team's strategy or execution — something important that isn't being done — and propose owning it.
task.howToDo: Write a 1-page proposal: gap, why it matters, what you'd do, resources needed.
guidelines: Come with a plan, not just an observation
task.suggestedStarter: I've noticed that we're not doing [X] — and here's why I think that matters and what I'd do about it.
task.successCriteria: You proposed filling a gap and received approval to act on it.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What made leadership say yes or no?

flashcards.title: Structure
flashcards.content: Gap → Why it matters → What I'd do → Resources needed.

activities.title: Lead a Product or Process Launch
activities.shortDescription: Own the end-to-end coordination of a launch from planning to delivery
activities.objective: Build end-to-end ownership and coordination skills
activities.motive: Launches are where initiative and execution intersect

task.whatToDo: Volunteer to lead the coordination of a product feature, process, or initiative launch.
task.howToDo: Build a launch plan with milestones, stakeholders, communication plan, and success criteria.
task.suggestedStarter: I'll own this launch — here's the plan I'm proposing.
task.successCriteria: The launch happened on time with documented success criteria met.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What would you do differently in the next launch?

flashcards.title: Launch Essentials
flashcards.content: Milestones / Stakeholders / Communication plan / Success criteria.

activities.title: Write an Annual Team Report
activities.shortDescription: Produce a document capturing your team's annual impact and learnings
leadershipPrinciples: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
activities.objective: Build organizational narrative skills and accountability
activities.motive: A team's impact is only visible if someone documents and shares it

task.whatToDo: Write an annual report for your team covering: accomplishments, challenges, learnings, and what's next.
task.howToDo: Use quantified impact wherever possible. Make it a story, not a list.
guidelines: Be honest about failures and learnings
task.suggestedStarter: In the past year, our team accomplished [X], navigated [Y], and learned [Z]. Here's what's next.
task.successCriteria: You published the report and it was referenced in at least one planning conversation.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did writing the annual report reveal about where the real value was created?

flashcards.title: Structure
flashcards.content: Accomplishments / Challenges / Learnings / What's next.

activities.title: Build a Knowledge Capture System for Your Team
activities.shortDescription: Design and implement a system to capture institutional knowledge
leadershipPrinciples: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
activities.objective: Build organizational knowledge infrastructure
activities.motive: Teams that systematically capture knowledge outperform those that rely on memory

task.whatToDo: Design and launch a knowledge management system for your team.
task.howToDo: Define categories, choose a tool, seed with 10 articles, and establish contribution norms.
guidelines: Make it easy to contribute to
task.suggestedStarter: Our team knowledge lives in too many heads — I want to fix that.
task.successCriteria: You launched a knowledge system with 10+ articles and 3+ team contributors in the first month.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What knowledge surprised you to realize was not written down?

flashcards.title: System
flashcards.content: A knowledge system only works if contributing to it is easy.

activities.title: Propose and Lead a Pilot Program
activities.shortDescription: Design a small-scale test of a new approach and run it
activities.objective: Build pilot program design and execution skills
activities.motive: Pilots de-risk new ideas by testing them before full commitment

task.whatToDo: Identify something new your team should try — a process, approach, or tool. Design a 4-week pilot.
task.howToDo: Define hypothesis, scope, success metrics, and what you'll do with the results.
task.suggestedStarter: I want to propose a 4-week pilot to test [X] — here's the design.
task.successCriteria: You ran the pilot, measured the outcomes, and presented a go/no-go recommendation.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did the pilot teach you that theory couldn't?

flashcards.title: Pilot Structure
flashcards.content: Hypothesis / Scope / Success Metric / Go-No-Go Recommendation.

activities.title: Present a Strategic Recommendation Unprompted
activities.shortDescription: Bring a strategic recommendation to leadership without being asked
activities.objective: Practice strategic initiative and leadership communication
activities.motive: The leaders who advance are those who see strategic opportunities before being asked to

task.whatToDo: Identify a strategic opportunity or gap and build a formal recommendation for leadership.
task.howToDo: Use: Situation → Complication → Question → Answer (SCQA) framework.
task.suggestedStarter: I've been thinking about [X] and I'd like to share a recommendation.
task.successCriteria: You presented an unsolicited strategic recommendation and received formal feedback.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What reaction told you the most about organizational priorities?

flashcards.title: SCQA
flashcards.content: Situation → Complication → Question → Answer.

activities.title: Lead a Cost Reduction Initiative
activities.shortDescription: Identify and drive a measurable reduction in team or operational costs
activities.objective: Build financial impact thinking into operational leadership
activities.motive: Professionals who improve economics become invaluable

task.whatToDo: Identify a cost or resource that your team is wasting and lead an initiative to reduce it by at least 20%.
task.howToDo: Baseline the cost, identify root causes, design reductions, implement, and measure.
guidelines: Involve those closest to the cost
task.suggestedStarter: I've identified [X] as a cost we can reduce — here's my approach.
task.successCriteria: You achieved at least a 20% measurable reduction in the targeted cost.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What was the root cause of the inefficiency?

flashcards.title: Process
flashcards.content: Baseline → Root cause → Reduce → Measure impact.

activities.title: Identify and Develop Yourself for a Future Role
activities.shortDescription: Create a development plan targeting a role you want in 1–2 years
activities.objective: Take ownership of your own career trajectory
activities.motive: Career growth is a personal responsibility, not a waiting game

task.whatToDo: Identify a role you want in 1–2 years and create a self-development plan to get there.
task.howToDo: Define the role, the gap between now and then, and specific actions to close it. Review monthly.
task.suggestedStarter: The role I'm building toward is [X]. Here's my gap analysis and plan.
task.successCriteria: You created a written plan and completed your first milestone within 30 days.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What surprised you about the gap between where you are and where you want to be?

flashcards.title: Process
flashcards.content: Target role → Gap analysis → 2–3 priority skills → Milestone plan.

activities.title: Lead an Organization-Wide Initiative
activities.shortDescription: Propose and lead a change that touches multiple teams or functions
activities.objective: Build the ability to drive change beyond your immediate scope
activities.motive: Organization-level impact requires organization-level initiative

task.whatToDo: Identify an organization-level improvement opportunity and lead it to implementation.
task.howToDo: Build a coalition, define scope, get executive sponsorship, and drive to completion.
task.suggestedStarter: I'd like to propose an initiative that could benefit all of [org area] — here's what I'm thinking.
task.successCriteria: The initiative was adopted across at least 3 teams with measurable outcomes.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What made coalition-building the hardest part?

flashcards.title: Key
flashcards.content: Organization-wide change needs executive sponsorship and broad communication.

activities.title: Design an Internship or Junior Program
activities.shortDescription: Create a structured development experience for early-career professionals
leadershipPrinciples: Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility
activities.objective: Build institutional talent development capabilities
activities.motive: Programs that develop early-career talent create compounding organizational value

task.whatToDo: Design a structured internship or junior professional program for your team or area.
task.howToDo: Define: objectives, rotation structure, mentor assignments, project scope, and evaluation criteria.
guidelines: Focus on their learning, not your task list
Create a community among the cohort
task.suggestedStarter: I want to design a program that actually develops the next generation — here's my proposal.
task.successCriteria: You designed the program and ran the first cohort with documented participant outcomes.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did participants say they got most from the program?

flashcards.title: Design Principle
flashcards.content: Programs that serve the learner's growth, not just the team's work, retain and develop talent.

activities.title: Conduct a Strategic Review of Your Function
activities.shortDescription: Lead a structured assessment of what your function should do more of, less of, and differently
activities.objective: Build strategic self-assessment skills at function level
activities.motive: Functions that review themselves critically improve. Those that don't become irrelevant.

task.whatToDo: Lead a structured strategic review of your team's or function's current activities and priorities.
task.howToDo: Assess: What's working? What isn't? What should we stop? What should we start? What's missing?
guidelines: Come out with a priority list
task.suggestedStarter: I want to take stock of what we're doing and whether we're doing the right things.
task.successCriteria: You completed the review and made 3 specific priority changes based on it.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What was the hardest thing to stop doing?

flashcards.title: Framework
flashcards.content: What's working / What isn't / Stop / Start / Missing.

activities.title: Define a 3-Year Vision for Your Area
activities.shortDescription: Articulate where your team or function should be in 3 years and what it takes to get there
activities.objective: Build long-horizon strategic leadership capability
activities.motive: The best leaders see further than their current quarter

task.whatToDo: Write and present a 3-year vision document for your team or functional area.
task.howToDo: Cover: where we are, where we need to be, what we must build, and what we must stop.
guidelines: Involve your team in building it
Present it to leadership for feedback
task.suggestedStarter: In 3 years, our team needs to be [X] to deliver [Y] for the organization.
task.successCriteria: You presented the 3-year vision to leadership and it was used in a planning conversation.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What assumptions are baked into the vision that you'd want to test?

flashcards.title: Vision Frame
flashcards.content: Where we are / Where we need to be / What we must build / What we must stop.

activities.title: Block Focus Time on Your Calendar
activities.shortDescription: Protect time for deep work by blocking your calendar
activities.objective: Build intentional time management habits
activities.motive: Your calendar is either controlled by you or controlled by others

task.whatToDo: Block 90 minutes of focused work time on your calendar every day for the next 5 days.
task.howToDo: Create a recurring block labeled 'Focus / Deep Work'. Treat it as a real meeting.
guidelines: Put it on your calendar today
Don't accept meetings in this slot lightly
Actually use it for deep work
task.suggestedStarter: Block: 'Deep Work — Do Not Disturb' on your calendar for 5 consecutive days.
task.successCriteria: You successfully protected and used focus time for 5 consecutive days.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How much more did you get done during protected focus time?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: If it's not on your calendar, it won't happen.

activities.title: End Each Day With a Shutdown Ritual
activities.shortDescription: Close your workday with a structured end-of-day review
activities.objective: Build sustainable, boundaried work habits
activities.motive: A shutdown ritual separates work and rest — making both better

task.whatToDo: End each workday with a 10-minute shutdown ritual for 5 days.
task.howToDo: Review today's progress, capture tomorrow's top 3 tasks, and declare work done.
guidelines: Do it at a consistent time
task.suggestedStarter: Today I completed X. Tomorrow's top 3 are Y, Z, A. Work done.
task.successCriteria: You completed a shutdown ritual for 5 consecutive days.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did having a shutdown ritual change how you felt at the start of the next day?

flashcards.title: Benefit
flashcards.content: A shutdown ritual tells your brain work is done — enabling real rest.

activities.title: Respond to Messages Within an Hour
activities.shortDescription: Build a reliable response time habit for professional communication
activities.objective: Build professional reliability through communication responsiveness
activities.motive: People trust those who are reliably responsive — even if the response is 'I'll get back to you'

task.whatToDo: For one week, respond to all work messages within 1 hour during working hours.
task.howToDo: If you can't act on it now, acknowledge it: 'Got this — I'll respond fully by [time].'
guidelines: Acknowledge fast; respond fully when ready
task.suggestedStarter: Got this — I'll come back to you by [time] with a full response.
task.successCriteria: You maintained sub-1-hour acknowledgment for all messages for 5 consecutive days.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did faster acknowledgment change how others worked with you?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Acknowledge fast. Respond fully when ready. Never leave people in silence.

activities.title: Keep a Daily Work Journal
activities.shortDescription: Write 5 minutes of notes at the end of each day
activities.objective: Build self-reflection habits that compound professional growth
activities.motive: Professionals who reflect on their work learn faster than those who just do it

task.whatToDo: Write 3–5 sentences in a work journal at the end of each day for 2 weeks.
task.howToDo: Cover: what you did, what you learned, and what you'll do differently tomorrow.
task.suggestedStarter: Today I: / I learned: / Tomorrow I'll try:
task.successCriteria: You journaled for 10 consecutive days and identified one recurring pattern.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What pattern did you find after 2 weeks that you wouldn't have noticed otherwise?

flashcards.title: Format
flashcards.content: Today I: / I learned: / Tomorrow I'll try:

activities.title: Prep for Every Meeting You Attend
activities.shortDescription: Read the agenda and prepare one contribution for every meeting
activities.objective: Build deliberate meeting preparation habits
activities.motive: Unprepared attendees are passengers. Prepared ones are participants.

task.whatToDo: Before every meeting for one week, read the agenda and prepare one specific contribution.
task.howToDo: Before each meeting, ask: What's the purpose? What can I add? What do I need to learn?
guidelines: Do this before every meeting, not some
task.suggestedStarter: Meeting: [Title]. My contribution: [X]. My question: [Y].
task.successCriteria: You prepared for and contributed to every meeting for 5 consecutive days.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did preparation change your experience and perceived value in meetings?

flashcards.title: Prep
flashcards.content: What's the purpose? What can I add? What do I need to learn?

activities.title: Review Your Priorities Every Monday Morning
activities.shortDescription: Start each week with a deliberate priority review
activities.objective: Build deliberate weekly planning habits
activities.motive: Professionals who plan their weeks accomplish more than those who react to them

task.whatToDo: Spend 15 minutes every Monday morning reviewing and setting your top 3 priorities for the week.
task.howToDo: Review what's due, what's important, and what needs progress. Write your top 3. Protect time for them.
task.suggestedStarter: Week of [date]: My top 3 priorities are: 1. 2. 3.
task.successCriteria: You completed your weekly priority review for 4 consecutive Mondays.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How often did your planned priorities match where you actually spent your time?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Top 3 priorities first, before the week fills itself up.

activities.title: Build a Second Brain for Your Work
activities.shortDescription: Create a personal knowledge management system for your professional life
activities.objective: Build infrastructure for sustained professional learning and productivity
activities.motive: Your memory is unreliable. A well-maintained system is not.

task.whatToDo: Set up a personal knowledge management system — a single place where you capture notes, learnings, and resources.
task.howToDo: Pick one tool (Notion, Obsidian, etc.). Create folders for: Projects, Learning, References, Meetings.
guidelines: Keep it simple — you'll abandon complex systems
task.suggestedStarter: I'm building a system to stop losing what I learn — here's how I'm organizing it.
task.successCriteria: You used your PKM system every day for 2 consecutive weeks.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What have you already captured that you would have forgotten without it?

flashcards.title: Categories
flashcards.content: Projects / Learning / References / Meetings.

activities.title: Track Your Own Performance Weekly
activities.shortDescription: Build a personal scorecard for your professional performance
activities.objective: Build self-directed performance accountability
activities.motive: Professionals who self-assess honestly grow faster than those who wait for reviews

task.whatToDo: Create a simple personal scorecard and score yourself on 5 key behaviors every week.
task.howToDo: Define 5 behaviors. Rate 1–5 each Friday. Review trends monthly.
guidelines: Be honest — no one else sees this
task.suggestedStarter: My 5 scorecard behaviors are: [X, Y, Z, A, B]. This week's scores: ...
task.successCriteria: You maintained a weekly scorecard for 4+ consecutive weeks.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What behavior was consistently lower than you expected?

flashcards.title: Purpose
flashcards.content: Self-assessment makes you your own most honest manager.

activities.title: Set Up a Weekly Review
activities.shortDescription: Run a structured review of the past week every Friday
activities.objective: Build a weekly rhythm of reflection and planning
activities.motive: Reflection is the multiplier of experience

task.whatToDo: Run a structured weekly review every Friday afternoon.
task.howToDo: Cover: Wins, challenges, lessons learned, and next week's priorities.
guidelines: Do it at a consistent time
Use it to plan the following week
task.suggestedStarter: Week of [date] Review: Wins: / Challenges: / Lessons: / Next week:
task.successCriteria: You ran a weekly review for 6 consecutive weeks.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What pattern emerged after 6 weeks that surprised you?

flashcards.title: Format
flashcards.content: Wins / Challenges / Lessons / Next week's top 3.

activities.title: Build a Reading Habit
activities.shortDescription: Read one professional book or 3 articles per week for a month
activities.objective: Build sustained professional learning habits
activities.motive: The most effective professionals are also the most deliberate learners

task.whatToDo: Commit to reading one professional book or 3 articles per week for a month.
task.howToDo: Schedule it. Take notes. Share one takeaway with your team each week.
guidelines: Schedule reading time like a meeting
Share at least one takeaway per week
task.suggestedStarter: This week I'm reading [X] — I'll share one takeaway by Friday.
task.successCriteria: You read consistently for 4 weeks and shared at least 4 takeaways with your team.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What reading changed how you approached your work this month?

flashcards.title: Habit
flashcards.content: Schedule reading. Share takeaways. Knowledge compounds.

activities.title: Eliminate One Time-Wasting Habit
activities.shortDescription: Identify and remove one habitual time drain from your work life
activities.duration: 5 mins to identify, ongoing to eliminate
activities.objective: Build intentional use of time by removing waste
activities.motive: Removing waste is as powerful as adding capability

task.whatToDo: Identify one habitual time drain and design a specific intervention to eliminate it.
task.howToDo: Track your time for 3 days. Identify the biggest waste. Design and implement one concrete fix.
guidelines: Be honest about what you're actually doing with your time
Make the fix concrete and measurable
task.suggestedStarter: After tracking my time, I've identified [X] as a major time drain — here's what I'm changing.
task.successCriteria: You reduced or eliminated the time drain by at least 50% over 2 weeks.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you do with the time you reclaimed?

flashcards.title: Process
flashcards.content: Track → Identify waste → Concrete fix → Measure change.

activities.title: Master Your Email Inbox
activities.shortDescription: Build a sustainable email management system
activities.objective: Build reliable communication management habits
activities.motive: An unmanaged inbox is a tax on your attention and reliability

task.whatToDo: Design and implement an email management system that gets your inbox under control.
task.howToDo: Use: Read, Process, Archive or Action. Set specific inbox check times. Never check on reflex.
guidelines: Aim for inbox zero at the end of each day
task.suggestedStarter: I'm committing to inbox zero every day — here's the system I'm using.
task.successCriteria: You maintained inbox zero (or near-zero) for 5 consecutive business days.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How much mental energy did you recover by not checking email reflexively?

flashcards.title: System
flashcards.content: Specific check times. Read → Process → Archive or Action. Never browse.

activities.title: Build a Monthly Learning Review
activities.shortDescription: Review what you've learned each month and identify what to learn next
activities.objective: Build systematic personal learning management
activities.motive: Learning that isn't reviewed is learning that doesn't stick

task.whatToDo: At the end of each month, review what you've learned and identify the top skill or topic for next month.
task.howToDo: Cover: What did I learn? What changed in my behavior? What's next?
guidelines: Be specific about next month's focus
task.suggestedStarter: This month I learned [X]. It changed how I do [Y]. Next month I'll focus on [Z].
task.successCriteria: You ran a monthly learning review for 3 consecutive months.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How much did you actually absorb from what you consumed?

flashcards.title: Format
flashcards.content: What I learned / How it changed my behavior / What I'll focus on next.

activities.title: Establish a Pre-Work Routine
activities.shortDescription: Build a consistent morning routine that prepares you to perform
activities.objective: Build consistent pre-work rituals that optimize performance
activities.motive: How you start work determines the quality of what follows

task.whatToDo: Design and commit to a consistent pre-work routine for 2 weeks.
task.howToDo: Include: physical movement, 10 minutes of planning, and eliminating the first distraction.
guidelines: Note the impact on your work energy
task.suggestedStarter: My pre-work routine: [physical activity] → [planning] → [deep work starts].
task.successCriteria: You maintained a consistent pre-work routine for 10 consecutive workdays.

activities.reflectionPrompt: How did starting work differently change the quality of your first hour?

flashcards.title: Principle
flashcards.content: How you start work shapes how the rest of it goes.

activities.title: Audit Your Commitments
activities.shortDescription: Review every commitment you've made and assess whether you should keep or renegotiate them
activities.objective: Build commitment hygiene — the discipline of managing what you've agreed to
activities.motive: Most professionals are over-committed without knowing it

task.whatToDo: List every commitment you currently have — projects, promises, recurring meetings, tasks.
task.howToDo: For each: Decide to Complete, Delegate, or Renegotiate. Take action on each.
guidelines: Renegotiate early — not after the deadline
task.suggestedStarter: Here is every commitment I currently hold. Here's what I'm doing with each.
task.successCriteria: You completed the commitment audit and took action on at least 30% of the items (complete, delegate, or renegotiate).

activities.reflectionPrompt: How many commitments had you forgotten you'd made?

flashcards.title: Three Options
flashcards.content: Complete / Delegate / Renegotiate. Choose one for every commitment.

activities.title: Set and Share Your Working Hours
activities.shortDescription: Explicitly communicate your working hours to your team
activities.objective: Build healthy professional boundaries through transparent communication
activities.motive: Unstated working hours lead to unstated expectations that erode wellbeing

task.whatToDo: Write and share your working hours and communication preferences with your team.
task.howToDo: State: when you're available, how to reach you urgently, and when you're not reachable.
guidelines: Stick to what you commit to
task.suggestedStarter: My working hours are [X]. For urgent matters: [Y]. I'm unavailable for messages after [Z].
task.successCriteria: You shared your working hours with your team and maintained them for 2 weeks.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Did others reciprocate? What changed in team communication quality?

flashcards.title: Why
flashcards.content: Stated working hours reduce anxiety for everyone — not just yourself.

activities.title: Design a Personal Work Productivity System
activities.shortDescription: Build a comprehensive, sustainable personal productivity system
activities.objective: Build infrastructure for sustained high performance
activities.motive: Improvising productivity daily is expensive. A designed system is not.

task.whatToDo: Design a full personal productivity system: task management, calendar management, and communication.
task.howToDo: Cover: how tasks are captured, prioritized, scheduled, and completed. Use a tool you'll actually stick to.
guidelines: Pick tools you'll use every day
task.suggestedStarter: My system: tasks → [tool]. Calendar: [rules]. Messages: [check times].
task.successCriteria: You used your system consistently for 4 weeks without reverting to old habits.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What was the biggest behavior change the system required of you?

flashcards.title: Principle
flashcards.content: The best productivity system is the one you actually use every day.

activities.title: Conduct a Time Audit
activities.shortDescription: Track every hour of your workday for a week and analyze what you find
activities.objective: Build self-awareness about actual time usage
activities.motive: Most professionals are wrong about where their time goes — a time audit reveals the truth

task.whatToDo: Track every work hour for 5 days. Categorize: deep work, shallow work, meetings, admin, reactive.
task.howToDo: Use a simple spreadsheet or time-tracking app. Analyze on Friday.
guidelines: Track in real time, not from memory
task.suggestedStarter: I'm tracking my time this week to understand where it actually goes.
task.successCriteria: You completed a full week time audit and identified 3 specific changes to make.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What surprised you most about where your time was going?

flashcards.title: Finding
flashcards.content: Most people are shocked by how little time they spend on high-leverage work.

activities.title: Do a Personal OKR Review
activities.shortDescription: Review your personal Objectives and Key Results quarterly
activities.objective: Build personal accountability through quarterly goal review
activities.motive: OKRs that are never reviewed are just good intentions

task.whatToDo: Conduct a quarterly review of your personal OKRs: score progress, analyze gaps, set next quarter.
task.howToDo: Score each KR 0–1. Write: what worked, what didn't, and what changes for Q+1.
guidelines: Set next quarter's OKRs immediately after
task.suggestedStarter: Q[X] OKR Review: O1: / KR Scores: / What worked: / What didn't: / Q[X+1] OKRs:
task.successCriteria: You completed a quarterly review and set next quarter's OKRs.

activities.reflectionPrompt: Where did you consistently overestimate your capacity?

flashcards.title: OKR Score
flashcards.content: 0.7 is a good OKR score. 1.0 means the goal was too easy.

activities.title: Build a Mentorship Relationship for Yourself
activities.shortDescription: Identify and establish a formal mentoring relationship
activities.objective: Build access to guidance that accelerates growth
activities.motive: The fastest path to expertise is often learning from someone who has it

task.whatToDo: Identify a mentor and formally establish a recurring relationship.
task.howToDo: Reach out, propose a format (monthly 30-minute calls), and prepare an agenda for the first session.
guidelines: Be specific about what you want to learn
Don't just show up — bring questions
task.suggestedStarter: I'd love to learn from your experience in [X] — would you be open to monthly calls?
task.successCriteria: You held at least 3 mentoring sessions and took at least one action from each.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What did you learn from your mentor that no training could have taught you?

flashcards.title: Rule
flashcards.content: Always come to a mentoring session with prepared questions — don't show up empty.

activities.title: Create a Personal Annual Review
activities.shortDescription: Write a comprehensive annual review of your professional year
activities.objective: Build systematic annual self-reflection and planning
activities.motive: Professionals who review the year seriously grow faster into the next

task.whatToDo: Write a comprehensive personal annual review.
task.howToDo: Cover: achievements, failures, relationships, learning, and priorities for next year.
task.suggestedStarter: In [year], I achieved [X], failed at [Y], learned [Z]. Next year's top priorities are: ...
task.successCriteria: You wrote and stored a complete annual review and defined 3 professional priorities for the coming year.

activities.reflectionPrompt: What would you tell your past self at the start of the year?

flashcards.title: Sections
flashcards.content: Achievements / Failures / Relationships / Learning / Next year's priorities.

activities.title: Establish a Professional Development Budget
